* Kagamitsukuri Jinja Torii.jpg {{commons category|Category:Kagamitsukuri-jinja}} [[JA:鏡作坐天照御魂神社]] {{wikidata link|Q38278496}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=鏡作坐天照御魂神社 |画像=[[Image:Kagamitsukuri Jinja Torii.jpg|270px]]
鳥居 |所在地={{jalink|奈良県|Q131287}}{{jalink|磯城郡|Q1155036}}{{jalink|田原本町|Q1203159}}八尾816 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{jalink|天火明命|Q10940411|天照国照彦火明命}}
{{jalink|イシコリドメ|Q6080112|石凝姥命}}
{{jalink|天糠戸}}命 |神体=三神二獣鏡 |社格={{jalink|式内社}}（大）
旧{{jalink|県社}} |創建={{jalink|崇神天皇|Q333530}}6年（{{jalink|紀元前92年|Q42350}}） |本殿=三間社{{jalink|春日造|Q3193781}} |別名=鏡作神社 |例祭={{jalink|10月25日|Q2954}} |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=鏡作坐天照
御魂神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''鏡作坐天照御魂神社'''（かがみつくりにますあまてるみたまじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|奈良県|Q131287}}{{jalink|磯城郡|Q1155036}}{{jalink|田原本町|Q1203159}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|式内大社|Q134917288}}で、旧{{jalink|社格|Q10444029}}は{{jalink|県社}}。4から5世紀にかけて鏡類を製作鋳造していた鏡作部がこの地一体に居住し、御鏡（{{jalink|天火明命|Q10940411|天照国照彦火明命}}）並びに遠祖（{{jalink|イシコリドメ|Q6080112|石凝姥命}}）を氏神として開いた神社であるとされる。 === 祭神 === 祭神は次の3柱。 * {{jalink|天火明命|Q10940411|天照国照彦火明命}}（あまてるくにてるひこほあかりのみこと） * {{jalink|イシコリドメ|Q6080112|石凝姥命}}（いしこりどめのみこと） * {{jalink|天糠戸}}命（あめのぬかどのみこと） {{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|神名帳}}における祭神の記載は1座であり、社名は「鏡作に鎮座する天照御魂神の社」という意味であることから、本来は「天照御魂神」を祀った{{jalink|太陽信仰}}の神社とされる。神名帳では、天照御魂神社と称する神社は当社以外にも{{jalink|日本|Q17}}各地に点在するが、これらは{{jalink|天照大神|Q455602}}（皇祖神）とは別の{{jalink|神格}}の{{jalink|太陽神|Q511056}}と考えられている。 === 歴史 === {{jalink|記紀|Q842308}}によると、{{jalink|崇神天皇|Q333530}}6年、{{jalink|皇居|Q500681}}内で祀られている{{jalink|天照大神|Q455602}}を畏れ多いとして笠縫邑の地に{{jalink|遷座}}したとし、社伝によると、「崇神天皇6年{{jalink|9月3日|Q2860}}、この地において日御像の鏡を（天照大神の代わりとして宮中で祀る為に）鋳造し、天照大神の御魂となす。今の内侍所の神鏡是なり。本社は其の（時に試作された）像鏡を{{jalink|天火明命|Q10940411|天照国照彦火明命}}として祀れるもので、この地を号して鏡作と言ふ」とある。この際に作られた試作の神鏡を御神体として祭祀し、天照大神が{{jalink|天岩戸|Q3231835}}に隠れた時に{{jalink|八咫鏡|Q2610017}}を作った遠祖である{{jalink|イシコリドメ|Q6080112|石凝姥命}}とその父{{jalink|天糠戸}}命をも併せて祀ったのが当社の起源とされる。『{{jalink|和名抄}}』には{{jalink|大和国|Q907495}}{{jalink|式下郡|Q11486987|城下郡}}の条に「鏡作郷」と記載があり、この地には仿製鏡の製作に従事した鏡作部（かがみつくりべ）が居住していたとされることから、当社の創建には鏡作部との関係が強いものと考えられる。更に近辺には{{jalink|鏡作伊多神社|Q54153278}}、{{jalink|鏡作麻気神社|Q43594607}}といった神社があり、当社との関連が指摘されている。 [[ファイル:鏡作坐天照御魂神社 御神宝.jpg|サムネイル|右]] {{jalink|大同 (日本)|Q1373191|大同}}元年（{{jalink|806年|Q30021}}）には{{jalink|神封|Q1689534}}十八戸が寄進されている（『{{jalink|新抄格勅符抄|Q11502312}}』）。『{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}』では大社に列しており、月次・{{jalink|新嘗祭|Q11501518|新嘗}}の{{jalink|奉幣|Q11445508}}に預ると記されている。 {{jalink|天安 (日本)|Q1075611|天安}}3年（{{jalink|859年|Q23897}}）{{jalink|1月27日|Q2284}}、従五位下から従五位上の{{jalink|神階|Q11591025}}を賜る （『{{jalink|日本三代実録|Q1052981}}』）- 表記は「鏡作天照御魂神」 中世以降は鏡作三所大明神と呼ばれていたが、{{jalink|江戸時代|Q184963}}に{{jalink|田原本藩|Q10425374}}（当時は{{jalink|交代寄合|Q11373913}}{{jalink|平野氏|Q11483690|平野家}}の{{jalink|知行地}}）が設置されるまでは荒廃していたという。江戸時代以降は鏡業界からの厚い信仰がある。 {{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}に入り{{jalink|神仏分離令}}が出されると、当社の{{jalink|神宮寺|Q1074971}}であった{{jalink|真言宗|Q550182}}飯趣山神宮寺聞楽院は廃寺となった。しかし、その鐘楼のみは残されて現在に至る。 {{jalink|1873年|Q7757}}（明治6年）4月に{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}に列し、{{jalink|1936年|Q18649}}（{{jalink|昭和|Q327567}}11年）には{{jalink|県社}}に列した。 神宝・三神二獣鏡がある。 === 境内 === [[File:Kagamitsukuri-jinja, honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|本殿]] [[File:Kagamitsukuri-jinja, Otauesai.jpg|thumb|220px|right|御田植祭]] * 本殿3棟 ** 第一殿 ** 第二殿 ** 第三殿 * 拝殿 * 鏡作坐{{jalink|若宮神社|Q11616280}} * {{jalink|天照大神|Q455602}}社 * {{jalink|手力雄神社|Q11496923}} * {{jalink|住吉神社|Q11381857}} * {{jalink|春日神社|Q10855011}} * 合祀社 - {{jalink|粟嶋神社}}、八意重金神社、{{jalink|保食神|Q2280323}}社、{{jalink|厳嶋神社}}、{{jalink|事代主神}}社、{{jalink|猿田彦神}}社、{{jalink|大国主神}}社 * {{jalink|金刀比羅神社|Q11646488}} - 石碑。 * 狭依姫神社 * 鍵取神社 * {{jalink|笛吹神社}} * {{jalink|愛宕社}} * 鏡池 * 鏡石 - 鏡池より出土したもの。鏡の製作時に鏡面を研磨する際に使用されたものという。 * 鐘楼 - {{jalink|神宮寺|Q1074971}}聞楽院の鐘楼。{{jalink|梵鐘|Q8192099}}は{{jalink|寛政|Q1195131}}7年（{{jalink|1795年|Q7815}}）に造られたもの。{{jalink|太平洋戦争|Q184425}}の時に{{jalink|金属類回収令|Q11646956}}によって供出させられたが、鋳つぶされる前に戦争が終わり無事に当社に返還された。 * 社務所 ファイル:Kagamitsukuri Jinja Haiden.jpg|拝殿 ファイル:Kagamitsukuri Jinja Kagamiishi.jpg|鏡石 ファイル:Kagamitsukuri Wakamiya Jinja.jpg|鏡作若宮神社 === 交通アクセス === * {{jalink|近畿日本鉄道|Q1531085|近鉄}}{{jalink|橿原線}}{{jalink|田原本駅|Q7689224}}から北東へ徒歩15分。 === 関連項目 === {{Commonscat}} * {{jalink|大和国の式内社一覧|Q11433427}} === 参考文献 === * 田原本町教育委員会　社頭案内板 === 外部リンク === '''鏡作坐天照御魂神社'''（かがみつくりにますあまてるみたまじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|奈良県|Q131287}}{{jalink|磯城郡|Q1155036}}{{jalink|田原本町|Q1203159}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|式内大社|Q134917288}}で、旧{{jalink|社格|Q10444029}}は{{jalink|県社}}。4から5世紀にかけて鏡類を製作鋳造していた鏡作部がこの地一体に居住し、御鏡（{{jalink|天火明命|Q10940411|天照国照彦火明命}}）並びに遠祖（{{jalink|イシコリドメ|Q6080112|石凝姥命}}）を氏神として開いた神社であるとされる。 === 祭神 === 祭神は次の3柱。 * {{jalink|天火明命|Q10940411|天照国照彦火明命}}（あまてるくにてるひこほあかりのみこと） * {{jalink|イシコリドメ|Q6080112|石凝姥命}}（いしこりどめのみこと） * {{jalink|天糠戸}}命（あめのぬかどのみこと） {{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|神名帳}}における祭神の記載は1座であり、社名は「鏡作に鎮座する天照御魂神の社」という意味であることから、本来は「天照御魂神」を祀った{{jalink|太陽信仰}}の神社とされる。神名帳では、天照御魂神社と称する神社は当社以外にも{{jalink|日本|Q17}}各地に点在するが、これらは{{jalink|天照大神|Q455602}}（皇祖神）とは別の{{jalink|神格}}の{{jalink|太陽神|Q511056}}と考えられている。 === 歴史 === {{jalink|記紀|Q842308}}によると、{{jalink|崇神天皇|Q333530}}6年、{{jalink|皇居|Q500681}}内で祀られている{{jalink|天照大神|Q455602}}を畏れ多いとして笠縫邑の地に{{jalink|遷座}}したとし、社伝によると、「崇神天皇6年{{jalink|9月3日|Q2860}}、この地において日御像の鏡を（天照大神の代わりとして宮中で祀る為に）鋳造し、天照大神の御魂となす。今の内侍所の神鏡是なり。本社は其の（時に試作された）像鏡を{{jalink|天火明命|Q10940411|天照国照彦火明命}}として祀れるもので、この地を号して鏡作と言ふ」とある。この際に作られた試作の神鏡を御神体として祭祀し、天照大神が{{jalink|天岩戸|Q3231835}}に隠れた時に{{jalink|八咫鏡|Q2610017}}を作った遠祖である{{jalink|イシコリドメ|Q6080112|石凝姥命}}とその父{{jalink|天糠戸}}命をも併せて祀ったのが当社の起源とされる。『{{jalink|和名抄}}』には{{jalink|大和国|Q907495}}{{jalink|式下郡|Q11486987|城下郡}}の条に「鏡作郷」と記載があり、この地には仿製鏡の製作に従事した鏡作部（かがみつくりべ）が居住していたとされることから、当社の創建には鏡作部との関係が強いものと考えられる。更に近辺には{{jalink|鏡作伊多神社|Q54153278}}、{{jalink|鏡作麻気神社|Q43594607}}といった神社があり、当社との関連が指摘されている。 [[ファイル:鏡作坐天照御魂神社 御神宝.jpg|サムネイル|右]] {{jalink|大同 (日本)|Q1373191|大同}}元年（{{jalink|806年|Q30021}}）には{{jalink|神封|Q1689534}}十八戸が寄進されている（『{{jalink|新抄格勅符抄|Q11502312}}』）。『{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}』では大社に列しており、月次・{{jalink|新嘗祭|Q11501518|新嘗}}の{{jalink|奉幣|Q11445508}}に預ると記されている。 {{jalink|天安 (日本)|Q1075611|天安}}3年（{{jalink|859年|Q23897}}）{{jalink|1月27日|Q2284}}、従五位下から従五位上の{{jalink|神階|Q11591025}}を賜る （『{{jalink|日本三代実録|Q1052981}}』）- 表記は「鏡作天照御魂神」 中世以降は鏡作三所大明神と呼ばれていたが、{{jalink|江戸時代|Q184963}}に{{jalink|田原本藩|Q10425374}}（当時は{{jalink|交代寄合|Q11373913}}{{jalink|平野氏|Q11483690|平野家}}の{{jalink|知行地}}）が設置されるまでは荒廃していたという。江戸時代以降は鏡業界からの厚い信仰がある。 {{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}に入り{{jalink|神仏分離令}}が出されると、当社の{{jalink|神宮寺|Q1074971}}であった{{jalink|真言宗|Q550182}}飯趣山神宮寺聞楽院は廃寺となった。しかし、その鐘楼のみは残されて現在に至る。 {{jalink|1873年|Q7757}}（明治6年）4月に{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}に列し、{{jalink|1936年|Q18649}}（{{jalink|昭和|Q327567}}11年）には{{jalink|県社}}に列した。 神宝・三神二獣鏡がある。 === 境内 === [[File:Kagamitsukuri-jinja, honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|本殿]] [[File:Kagamitsukuri-jinja, Otauesai.jpg|thumb|220px|right|御田植祭]] * 本殿3棟 ** 第一殿 ** 第二殿 ** 第三殿 * 拝殿 * 鏡作坐{{jalink|若宮神社|Q11616280}} * {{jalink|天照大神|Q455602}}社 * {{jalink|手力雄神社|Q11496923}} * {{jalink|住吉神社|Q11381857}} * {{jalink|春日神社|Q10855011}} * 合祀社 - {{jalink|粟嶋神社}}、八意重金神社、{{jalink|保食神|Q2280323}}社、{{jalink|厳嶋神社}}、{{jalink|事代主神}}社、{{jalink|猿田彦神}}社、{{jalink|大国主神}}社 * {{jalink|金刀比羅神社|Q11646488}} - 石碑。 * 狭依姫神社 * 鍵取神社 * {{jalink|笛吹神社}} * {{jalink|愛宕社}} * 鏡池 * 鏡石 - 鏡池より出土したもの。鏡の製作時に鏡面を研磨する際に使用されたものという。 * 鐘楼 - {{jalink|神宮寺|Q1074971}}聞楽院の鐘楼。{{jalink|梵鐘|Q8192099}}は{{jalink|寛政|Q1195131}}7年（{{jalink|1795年|Q7815}}）に造られたもの。{{jalink|太平洋戦争|Q184425}}の時に{{jalink|金属類回収令|Q11646956}}によって供出させられたが、鋳つぶされる前に戦争が終わり無事に当社に返還された。 * Kagamitsukuri Jinja Torii.jpg {{commons category|Category:Kagamitsukuri-jinja}} [[JA:鏡作坐天照御魂神社]] {{wikidata link|Q38278496}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=鏡作坐天照御魂神社 |画像=[[Image:Kagamitsukuri Jinja Torii.jpg|270px]]
鳥居 |所在地={{jalink|奈良県|Q131287}}{{jalink|磯城郡|Q1155036}}{{jalink|田原本町|Q1203159}}八尾816 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{jalink|天火明命|Q10940411|天照国照彦火明命}}
{{jalink|イシコリドメ|Q6080112|石凝姥命}}
{{jalink|天糠戸}}命 |神体=三神二獣鏡 |社格={{jalink|式内社}}（大）
旧{{jalink|県社}} |創建={{jalink|崇神天皇|Q333530}}6年（{{jalink|紀元前92年|Q42350}}） |本殿=三間社{{jalink|春日造|Q3193781}} |別名=鏡作神社 |例祭={{jalink|10月25日|Q2954}} |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=鏡作坐天照
御魂神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''鏡作坐天照御魂神社'''（かがみつくりにますあまてるみたまじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|奈良県|Q131287}}{{jalink|磯城郡|Q1155036}}{{jalink|田原本町|Q1203159}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|式内大社|Q134917288}}で、旧{{jalink|社格|Q10444029}}は{{jalink|県社}}。4から5世紀にかけて鏡類を製作鋳造していた鏡作部がこの地一体に居住し、御鏡（{{jalink|天火明命|Q10940411|天照国照彦火明命}}）並びに遠祖（{{jalink|イシコリドメ|Q6080112|石凝姥命}}）を氏神として開いた神社であるとされる。 === 祭神 === 祭神は次の3柱。 * {{jalink|天火明命|Q10940411|天照国照彦火明命}}（あまてるくにてるひこほあかりのみこと） * {{jalink|イシコリドメ|Q6080112|石凝姥命}}（いしこりどめのみこと） * {{jalink|天糠戸}}命（あめのぬかどのみこと） {{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|神名帳}}における祭神の記載は1座であり、社名は「鏡作に鎮座する天照御魂神の社」という意味であることから、本来は「天照御魂神」を祀った{{jalink|太陽信仰}}の神社とされる。神名帳では、天照御魂神社と称する神社は当社以外にも{{jalink|日本|Q17}}各地に点在するが、これらは{{jalink|天照大神|Q455602}}（皇祖神）とは別の{{jalink|神格}}の{{jalink|太陽神|Q511056}}と考えられている。 === 歴史 === {{jalink|記紀|Q842308}}によると、{{jalink|崇神天皇|Q333530}}6年、{{jalink|皇居|Q500681}}内で祀られている{{jalink|天照大神|Q455602}}を畏れ多いとして笠縫邑の地に{{jalink|遷座}}したとし、社伝によると、「崇神天皇6年{{jalink|9月3日|Q2860}}、この地において日御像の鏡を（天照大神の代わりとして宮中で祀る為に）鋳造し、天照大神の御魂となす。今の内侍所の神鏡是なり。本社は其の（時に試作された）像鏡を{{jalink|天火明命|Q10940411|天照国照彦火明命}}として祀れるもので、この地を号して鏡作と言ふ」とある。この際に作られた試作の神鏡を御神体として祭祀し、天照大神が{{jalink|天岩戸|Q3231835}}に隠れた時に{{jalink|八咫鏡|Q2610017}}を作った遠祖である{{jalink|イシコリドメ|Q6080112|石凝姥命}}とその父{{jalink|天糠戸}}命をも併せて祀ったのが当社の起源とされる。『{{jalink|和名抄}}』には{{jalink|大和国|Q907495}}{{jalink|式下郡|Q11486987|城下郡}}の条に「鏡作郷」と記載があり、この地には仿製鏡の製作に従事した鏡作部（かがみつくりべ）が居住していたとされることから、当社の創建には鏡作部との関係が強いものと考えられる。更に近辺には{{jalink|鏡作伊多神社|Q54153278}}、{{jalink|鏡作麻気神社|Q43594607}}といった神社があり、当社との関連が指摘されている。 [[ファイル:鏡作坐天照御魂神社 御神宝.jpg|サムネイル|右]] {{jalink|大同 (日本)|Q1373191|大同}}元年（{{jalink|806年|Q30021}}）には{{jalink|神封|Q1689534}}十八戸が寄進されている（『{{jalink|新抄格勅符抄|Q11502312}}』）。『{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}』では大社に列しており、月次・{{jalink|新嘗祭|Q11501518|新嘗}}の{{jalink|奉幣|Q11445508}}に預ると記されている。 {{jalink|天安 (日本)|Q1075611|天安}}3年（{{jalink|859年|Q23897}}）{{jalink|1月27日|Q2284}}、従五位下から従五位上の{{jalink|神階|Q11591025}}を賜る （『{{jalink|日本三代実録|Q1052981}}』）- 表記は「鏡作天照御魂神」 中世以降は鏡作三所大明神と呼ばれていたが、{{jalink|江戸時代|Q184963}}に{{jalink|田原本藩|Q10425374}}（当時は{{jalink|交代寄合|Q11373913}}{{jalink|平野氏|Q11483690|平野家}}の{{jalink|知行地}}）が設置されるまでは荒廃していたという。江戸時代以降は鏡業界からの厚い信仰がある。 {{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}に入り{{jalink|神仏分離令}}が出されると、当社の{{jalink|神宮寺|Q1074971}}であった{{jalink|真言宗|Q550182}}飯趣山神宮寺聞楽院は廃寺となった。しかし、その鐘楼のみは残されて現在に至る。 {{jalink|1873年|Q7757}}（明治6年）4月に{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}に列し、{{jalink|1936年|Q18649}}（{{jalink|昭和|Q327567}}11年）には{{jalink|県社}}に列した。 神宝・三神二獣鏡がある。 === 境内 === [[File:Kagamitsukuri-jinja, honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|本殿]] [[File:Kagamitsukuri-jinja, Otauesai.jpg|thumb|220px|right|御田植祭]] * 本殿3棟 ** 第一殿 ** 第二殿 ** 第三殿 * 拝殿 * 鏡作坐{{jalink|若宮神社|Q11616280}} * {{jalink|天照大神|Q455602}}社 * {{jalink|手力雄神社|Q11496923}} * {{jalink|住吉神社|Q11381857}} * {{jalink|春日神社|Q10855011}} * 合祀社 - {{jalink|粟嶋神社}}、八意重金神社、{{jalink|保食神|Q2280323}}社、{{jalink|厳嶋神社}}、{{jalink|事代主神}}社、{{jalink|猿田彦神}}社、{{jalink|大国主神}}社 * {{jalink|金刀比羅神社|Q11646488}} - 石碑。 * 狭依姫神社 * 鍵取神社 * {{jalink|笛吹神社}} * {{jalink|愛宕社}} * 鏡池 * 鏡石 - 鏡池より出土したもの。鏡の製作時に鏡面を研磨する際に使用されたものという。 * 鐘楼 - {{jalink|神宮寺|Q1074971}}聞楽院の鐘楼。{{jalink|梵鐘|Q8192099}}は{{jalink|寛政|Q1195131}}7年（{{jalink|1795年|Q7815}}）に造られたもの。{{jalink|太平洋戦争|Q184425}}の時に{{jalink|金属類回収令|Q11646956}}によって供出させられたが、鋳つぶされる前に戦争が終わり無事に当社に返還された。 * 社務所 ファイル:Kagamitsukuri Jinja Haiden.jpg|拝殿 ファイル:Kagamitsukuri Jinja Kagamiishi.jpg|鏡石 ファイル:Kagamitsukuri Wakamiya Jinja.jpg|鏡作若宮神社 === 交通アクセス === * {{jalink|近畿日本鉄道|Q1531085|近鉄}}{{jalink|橿原線}}{{jalink|田原本駅|Q7689224}}から北東へ徒歩15分。 === 関連項目 === {{Commonscat}} * {{jalink|大和国の式内社一覧|Q11433427}} === 参考文献 === * 田原本町教育委員会　社頭案内板 === 外部リンク === *[http://www.town.tawaramoto.nara.jp/kanko/bunkazai/temple/3446.html 鏡作神社] - 田原本町 *[http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22807 鏡作坐天照御魂神社] - 國學院大學21世紀COEプログラム「神道・神社史料集成」 {{神道 横}} * Kagamitsukuri Jinja Torii.jpg {{commons category|Category:Kagamitsukuri-jinja}} [[JA:鏡作坐天照御魂神社]] {{wikidata link|Q38278496}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Kagamitsukuri Nimasu Amateru Mitama Shrine |画像=[[Image:Kagamitsukuri Jinja Torii.jpg|270px]]
Torii |所在地={{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}{{jalink|Shiki District|Q1155036}}{{jalink|Tawaramoto|Q1203159}} Yao 816 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{jalink|Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|Amateru Kuniteru Hikohokari-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Ishikoridome|Q6080112|Ishikoridome-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Ame-no-Nukado}} no Mikoto |神体=Three divine mirrors with two beasts |社格={{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (major)
former {{jalink|kensha}} |創建={{jalink|Emperor Sujin|Q333530}} 6 ({{jalink|92 BCE|Q42350}}) |本殿=Three-bay shrine, {{jalink|kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781}} |別名=Kagamitsukuri Shrine |例祭={{jalink|October 25|Q2954}} |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Kagamitsukuri Nimasu Amateru
Mitama Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kagamitsukuri Nimasu Amateru Mitama Shrine''' (かがみつくりにますあまてるみたまじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Tawaramoto|Q1203159}}, {{jalink|Shiki District|Q1155036}}, {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}. It is a {{jalink|major Shikinaisha|Q134917288}}, and its former {{jalink|shrine rank|Q10444029}} was {{jalink|kensha}}. It is said to have been founded by the Kagamitsukuri-be mirror-making clan, who lived in this area from the 4th to 5th centuries producing mirrors, and enshrined their tutelary deity ({{jalink|Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|Amateru Kuniteru Hikohokari-no-Mikoto}}) together with their ancestor ({{jalink|Ishikoridome|Q6080112|Ishikoridome-no-Mikoto}}). === Enshrined deities === The three enshrined deities are: * {{jalink|Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|Amateru Kuniteru Hikohokari-no-Mikoto}} (あまてるくにてるひこほあかりのみこと) * {{jalink|Ishikoridome|Q6080112|Ishikoridome-no-Mikoto}} (いしこりどめのみこと) * {{jalink|Ame-no-Nukado}} no Mikoto (あめのぬかどのみこと) In the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|Register of Shrines}}, only one deity seat is recorded. Because the shrine name means "the shrine of Amateru Mitama no Kami seated at Kagamitsukuri," it is considered originally a solar-cult shrine devoted to "Amateru Mitama no Kami." In the Jinmyocho, shrines called Amateru Mitama Jinja exist in several regions of {{jalink|Japan|Q17}}; these are regarded as solar deities of a different {{jalink|divine status}} from {{jalink|Amaterasu|Q455602}} (the imperial ancestral deity). === History === According to classical chronicles ({{jalink|Kiki|Q842308}}), in the 6th year of {{jalink|Emperor Sujin|Q333530}}, the deity {{jalink|Amaterasu|Q455602}} enshrined in the {{jalink|Imperial Palace|Q500681}} was moved to the land of Sasanuhi because of reverence. Shrine tradition states: "On the 3rd day of the 9th month in Emperor Sujin's 6th year, mirrors in the form of the sun image were cast in this place (to enshrine in the palace in place of Amaterasu), and made into the divine spirit of Amaterasu; the mirror now in the inner attendants' office is that one. This shrine enshrines the prototype mirror made at that time as {{jalink|Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|Amateru Kuniteru Hikohokari-no-Mikoto}}, and this place is therefore called Kagamitsukuri." It is said that this prototype mirror became the shrine's sacred object, and that {{jalink|Ishikoridome|Q6080112|Ishikoridome-no-Mikoto}} (who forged the {{jalink|Yata no Kagami|Q2610017}} when Amaterasu hid in the {{jalink|Ame-no-Iwato|Q3231835}}) and her father {{jalink|Ame-no-Nukado}} no Mikoto were also enshrined here. The ''{{jalink|Wamyosho}}'' records "Kagamitsukuri-go" in {{jalink|Yamato Province|Q907495}}, {{jalink|Shikige District|Q11486987|Jokage District}}. Since the mirror-making clan (kagamitsukuri-be) is said to have lived here producing mirrors, the shrine's founding is thought to be closely tied to that clan. Nearby shrines such as {{jalink|Kagamitsukuri Ita Shrine|Q54153278}} and {{jalink|Kagamitsukuri Maki Shrine|Q43594607}} are also pointed out as related. [[ファイル:鏡作坐天照御魂神社 御神宝.jpg|サムネイル|右]] In {{jalink|Daido (Japan)|Q1373191|Daido}} 1 ({{jalink|806|Q30021}}), eighteen households were donated as shrine estates (''{{jalink|Shinshoku Kofu Sho|Q11502312}}''). In the ''{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932}}'' it is listed as a major shrine and noted to receive offerings for monthly rites and the {{jalink|Niiname-sai|Q11501518|new harvest rite}}. On {{jalink|January 27|Q2284}}, {{jalink|Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|Ten'an}} 3 ({{jalink|859|Q23897}}), it was granted divine rank from junior fifth lower to junior fifth upper (''{{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}'') - recorded as "Kagamitsukuri Amateru Mitama no Kami." From the medieval period onward it was called "Kagamitsukuri Sansho Daimyojin," but is said to have declined until the {{jalink|Tawaramoto Domain|Q10425374}} was established in the {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}} (then a {{jalink|kōtai yoriai|Q11373913}} holding of the {{jalink|Hirano clan|Q11483690|Hirano family}}). From the Edo period onward, it drew deep devotion from the mirror industry. After entering the {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} era and issuance of the {{jalink|Shinbutsu Bunri Edict}}, the shrine temple, the {{jalink|Shingon sect|Q550182}} Kangakurin of Iishu-zan Jingū-ji, was abolished. However, its bell tower alone remained and survives to the present. In April {{jalink|1873|Q7757}} (Meiji 6) it was ranked as a {{jalink|gōsha|Q134917316}}, and in {{jalink|1936|Q18649}} ({{jalink|Showa|Q327567}} 11) it was ranked as {{jalink|kensha}}. It possesses the sacred treasure called "three divine mirrors with two beasts." === Precinct === [[File:Kagamitsukuri-jinja, honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|Main hall]] [[File:Kagamitsukuri-jinja, Otauesai.jpg|thumb|220px|right|Rice-planting festival]] * 3 main halls ** First hall ** Second hall ** Third hall * Haiden * Kagamitsukuri-za {{jalink|Wakamiya Shrine|Q11616280}} * {{jalink|Amaterasu|Q455602}} Shrine * {{jalink|Tajikarao Shrine|Q11496923}} * {{jalink|Sumiyoshi Shrine|Q11381857}} * {{jalink|Kasuga Shrine|Q10855011}} * Gosha-sha (combined shrine) - {{jalink|Kasu嶋 Shrine}}, Hachiekanekami Shrine, {{jalink|Ukemochi|Q2280323}} Shrine, {{jalink|Itsukushima Shrine}}, {{jalink|Kotoshironushi}} Shrine, {{jalink|Sarutahiko}} Shrine, {{jalink|Ōkuninushi}} Shrine * {{jalink|Kotohira Shrine|Q11646488}} - stone monument * Sayorihime Shrine * Kagitori Shrine * {{jalink|Fuefuki Shrine}} * {{jalink|Aizome Shrine}} * Kagami Pond * Kagami Stone - unearthed from Kagami Pond. It is said to have been used to polish mirror surfaces during mirror making. * Bell tower - bell tower of Jingū-ji Kangakurin. The {{jalink|bonsho|Q8192099}} was made in {{jalink|Kansei|Q1195131}} 7 ({{jalink|1795|Q7815}}). During the {{jalink|Pacific War|Q184425}} it was surrendered under the {{jalink|metal collection order|Q11646956}}, but the war ended before it was melted and it was safely returned to the shrine. * Shrine office ファイル:Kagamitsukuri Jinja Haiden.jpg|Haiden ファイル:Kagamitsukuri Jinja Kagamiishi.jpg|Kagami Stone ファイル:Kagamitsukuri Wakamiya Jinja.jpg|Kagamitsukuri Wakamiya Shrine === Access === * 15 minutes on foot northeast from {{jalink|Tawaramoto Station|Q7689224}} on the {{jalink|Kintetsu|Q1531085|Kintetsu}} {{jalink|Kashihara Line}}. === See also === {{Commonscat}} * {{jalink|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|Q11433427}} === References === * Information board by Tawaramoto Board of Education === External links === *[http://www.town.tawaramoto.nara.jp/kanko/bunkazai/temple/3446.html Kagamitsukuri Shrine] - Tawaramoto Town *[http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22807 Kagamitsukuri Nimasu Amateru Mitama Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Compilation of Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials" {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かかみつくりにますあまてるみたましんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kagamitsukuri Nimasu Amateru Mitama Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kagamitsukuri Nimasu Amateru Mitama Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧県社]] [[Category:田原本町の建築物]] [[Category:田原本町の歴史]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:need translation]]

* Kagamitsukuri Jinja Torii.jpg {{commons category|Category:Kagamitsukuri-jinja}} [[JA:鏡作坐天照御魂神社]] {{wikidata link|Q38278496}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Kagamitsukuri Nimasu Amateru Mitama Shrine |画像=[[Image:Kagamitsukuri Jinja Torii.jpg|270px]]
Torii |所在地={{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}{{jalink|Shiki District|Q1155036}}{{jalink|Tawaramoto|Q1203159}} Yao 816 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{jalink|Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|Amateru Kuniteru Hikohokari-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Ishikoridome|Q6080112|Ishikoridome-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Ame-no-Nukado}} no Mikoto |神体=Three divine mirrors with two beasts |社格={{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (major)
former {{jalink|kensha}} |創建={{jalink|Emperor Sujin|Q333530}} 6 ({{jalink|92 BCE|Q42350}}) |本殿=Three-bay shrine, {{jalink|kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781}} |別名=Kagamitsukuri Shrine |例祭={{jalink|October 25|Q2954}} |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Kagamitsukuri Nimasu Amateru
Mitama Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kagamitsukuri Nimasu Amateru Mitama Shrine''' (かがみつくりにますあまてるみたまじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Tawaramoto|Q1203159}}, {{jalink|Shiki District|Q1155036}}, {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}. It is a {{jalink|major Shikinaisha|Q134917288}}, and its former {{jalink|shrine rank|Q10444029}} was {{jalink|kensha}}. It is said to have been founded by the Kagamitsukuri-be mirror-making clan, who lived in this area from the 4th to 5th centuries producing mirrors, and enshrined their tutelary deity ({{jalink|Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|Amateru Kuniteru Hikohokari-no-Mikoto}}) together with their ancestor ({{jalink|Ishikoridome|Q6080112|Ishikoridome-no-Mikoto}}). === Enshrined deities === The three enshrined deities are: * {{jalink|Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|Amateru Kuniteru Hikohokari-no-Mikoto}} (あまてるくにてるひこほあかりのみこと) * {{jalink|Ishikoridome|Q6080112|Ishikoridome-no-Mikoto}} (いしこりどめのみこと) * {{jalink|Ame-no-Nukado}} no Mikoto (あめのぬかどのみこと) In the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|Register of Shrines}}, only one deity seat is recorded. Because the shrine name means "the shrine of Amateru Mitama no Kami seated at Kagamitsukuri," it is considered originally a solar-cult shrine devoted to "Amateru Mitama no Kami." In the Jinmyocho, shrines called Amateru Mitama Jinja exist in several regions of {{jalink|Japan|Q17}}; these are regarded as solar deities of a different {{jalink|divine status}} from {{jalink|Amaterasu|Q455602}} (the imperial ancestral deity). === History === According to classical chronicles ({{jalink|Kiki|Q842308}}), in the 6th year of {{jalink|Emperor Sujin|Q333530}}, the deity {{jalink|Amaterasu|Q455602}} enshrined in the {{jalink|Imperial Palace|Q500681}} was moved to the land of Sasanuhi because of reverence. Shrine tradition states: "On the 3rd day of the 9th month in Emperor Sujin's 6th year, mirrors in the form of the sun image were cast in this place (to enshrine in the palace in place of Amaterasu), and made into the divine spirit of Amaterasu; the mirror now in the inner attendants' office is that one. This shrine enshrines the prototype mirror made at that time as {{jalink|Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|Amateru Kuniteru Hikohokari-no-Mikoto}}, and this place is therefore called Kagamitsukuri." It is said that this prototype mirror became the shrine's sacred object, and that {{jalink|Ishikoridome|Q6080112|Ishikoridome-no-Mikoto}} (who forged the {{jalink|Yata no Kagami|Q2610017}} when Amaterasu hid in the {{jalink|Ame-no-Iwato|Q3231835}}) and her father {{jalink|Ame-no-Nukado}} no Mikoto were also enshrined here. The ''{{jalink|Wamyosho}}'' records "Kagamitsukuri-go" in {{jalink|Yamato Province|Q907495}}, {{jalink|Shikige District|Q11486987|Jokage District}}. Since the mirror-making clan (kagamitsukuri-be) is said to have lived here producing mirrors, the shrine's founding is thought to be closely tied to that clan. Nearby shrines such as {{jalink|Kagamitsukuri Ita Shrine|Q54153278}} and {{jalink|Kagamitsukuri Maki Shrine|Q43594607}} are also pointed out as related. [[ファイル:鏡作坐天照御魂神社 御神宝.jpg|サムネイル|右]] In {{jalink|Daido (Japan)|Q1373191|Daido}} 1 ({{jalink|806|Q30021}}), eighteen households were donated as shrine estates (''{{jalink|Shinshoku Kofu Sho|Q11502312}}''). In the ''{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932}}'' it is listed as a major shrine and noted to receive offerings for monthly rites and the {{jalink|Niiname-sai|Q11501518|new harvest rite}}. On {{jalink|January 27|Q2284}}, {{jalink|Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|Ten'an}} 3 ({{jalink|859|Q23897}}), it was granted divine rank from junior fifth lower to junior fifth upper (''{{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}'') - recorded as "Kagamitsukuri Amateru Mitama no Kami." From the medieval period onward it was called "Kagamitsukuri Sansho Daimyojin," but is said to have declined until the {{jalink|Tawaramoto Domain|Q10425374}} was established in the {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}} (then a {{jalink|kōtai yoriai|Q11373913}} holding of the {{jalink|Hirano clan|Q11483690|Hirano family}}). From the Edo period onward, it drew deep devotion from the mirror industry. After entering the {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} era and issuance of the {{jalink|Shinbutsu Bunri Edict}}, the shrine temple, the {{jalink|Shingon sect|Q550182}} Kangakurin of Iishu-zan Jingū-ji, was abolished. However, its bell tower alone remained and survives to the present. In April {{jalink|1873|Q7757}} (Meiji 6) it was ranked as a {{jalink|gōsha|Q134917316}}, and in {{jalink|1936|Q18649}} ({{jalink|Showa|Q327567}} 11) it was ranked as {{jalink|kensha}}. It possesses the sacred treasure called "three divine mirrors with two beasts." === Precinct === [[File:Kagamitsukuri-jinja, honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|Main hall]] [[File:Kagamitsukuri-jinja, Otauesai.jpg|thumb|220px|right|Rice-planting festival]] * 3 main halls ** First hall ** Second hall ** Third hall * Haiden * Kagamitsukuri-za {{jalink|Wakamiya Shrine|Q11616280}} * {{jalink|Amaterasu|Q455602}} Shrine * {{jalink|Tajikarao Shrine|Q11496923}} * {{jalink|Sumiyoshi Shrine|Q11381857}} * {{jalink|Kasuga Shrine|Q10855011}} * Gosha-sha (combined shrine) - {{jalink|Kasu嶋 Shrine}}, Hachiekanekami Shrine, {{jalink|Ukemochi|Q2280323}} Shrine, {{jalink|Itsukushima Shrine}}, {{jalink|Kotoshironushi}} Shrine, {{jalink|Sarutahiko}} Shrine, {{jalink|Ōkuninushi}} Shrine * {{jalink|Kotohira Shrine|Q11646488}} - stone monument * Sayorihime Shrine * Kagitori Shrine * {{jalink|Fuefuki Shrine}} * {{jalink|Aizome Shrine}} * Kagami Pond * Kagami Stone - unearthed from Kagami Pond. It is said to have been used to polish mirror surfaces during mirror making. * Bell tower - bell tower of Jingū-ji Kangakurin. The {{jalink|bonsho|Q8192099}} was made in {{jalink|Kansei|Q1195131}} 7 ({{jalink|1795|Q7815}}). During the {{jalink|Pacific War|Q184425}} it was surrendered under the {{jalink|metal collection order|Q11646956}}, but the war ended before it was melted and it was safely returned to the shrine. * Shrine office ファイル:Kagamitsukuri Jinja Haiden.jpg|Haiden ファイル:Kagamitsukuri Jinja Kagamiishi.jpg|Kagami Stone ファイル:Kagamitsukuri Wakamiya Jinja.jpg|Kagamitsukuri Wakamiya Shrine === Access === * 15 minutes on foot northeast from {{jalink|Tawaramoto Station|Q7689224}} on the {{jalink|Kintetsu|Q1531085|Kintetsu}} {{jalink|Kashihara Line}}. === See also === {{Commonscat}} * {{jalink|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|Q11433427}} === References === * Information board by Tawaramoto Board of Education === External links === *[http://www.town.tawaramoto.nara.jp/kanko/bunkazai/temple/3446.html Kagamitsukuri Shrine] - Tawaramoto Town *[http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22807 Kagamitsukuri Nimasu Amateru Mitama Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Compilation of Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials" {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かかみつくりにますあまてるみたましんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kagamitsukuri Nimasu Amateru Mitama Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kagamitsukuri Nimasu Amateru Mitama Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧県社]] [[Category:田原本町の建築物]] [[Category:田原本町の歴史]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:need translation]]

* Kagamitsukuri Jinja Torii.jpg {{commons category|Category:Kagamitsukuri-jinja}} [[JA:鏡作坐天照御魂神社]] {{wikidata link|Q38278496}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Kagamitsukuri Nimasu Amateru Mitama Shrine |画像=[[Image:Kagamitsukuri Jinja Torii.jpg|270px]]
Torii |所在地={{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}{{jalink|Shiki District|Q1155036}}{{jalink|Tawaramoto|Q1203159}} Yao 816 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{jalink|Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|Amateru Kuniteru Hikohokari-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Ishikoridome|Q6080112|Ishikoridome-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Ame-no-Nukado}} no Mikoto |神体=Three divine mirrors with two beasts |社格={{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (major)
former {{jalink|kensha}} |創建={{jalink|Emperor Sujin|Q333530}} 6 ({{jalink|92 BCE|Q42350}}) |本殿=Three-bay shrine, {{jalink|kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781}} |別名=Kagamitsukuri Shrine |例祭={{jalink|October 25|Q2954}} |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Kagamitsukuri Nimasu Amateru
Mitama Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kagamitsukuri Nimasu Amateru Mitama Shrine''' (かがみつくりにますあまてるみたまじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Tawaramoto|Q1203159}}, {{jalink|Shiki District|Q1155036}}, {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}. It is a {{jalink|major Shikinaisha|Q134917288}}, and its former {{jalink|shrine rank|Q10444029}} was {{jalink|kensha}}. It is said to have been founded by the Kagamitsukuri-be mirror-making clan, who lived in this area from the 4th to 5th centuries producing mirrors, and enshrined their tutelary deity ({{jalink|Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|Amateru Kuniteru Hikohokari-no-Mikoto}}) together with their ancestor ({{jalink|Ishikoridome|Q6080112|Ishikoridome-no-Mikoto}}). === Enshrined deities === The three enshrined deities are: * {{jalink|Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|Amateru Kuniteru Hikohokari-no-Mikoto}} (あまてるくにてるひこほあかりのみこと) * {{jalink|Ishikoridome|Q6080112|Ishikoridome-no-Mikoto}} (いしこりどめのみこと) * {{jalink|Ame-no-Nukado}} no Mikoto (あめのぬかどのみこと) In the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|Register of Shrines}}, only one deity seat is recorded. Because the shrine name means "the shrine of Amateru Mitama no Kami seated at Kagamitsukuri," it is considered originally a solar-cult shrine devoted to "Amateru Mitama no Kami." In the Jinmyocho, shrines called Amateru Mitama Jinja exist in several regions of {{jalink|Japan|Q17}}; these are regarded as solar deities of a different {{jalink|divine status}} from {{jalink|Amaterasu|Q455602}} (the imperial ancestral deity). === History === According to classical chronicles ({{jalink|Kiki|Q842308}}), in the 6th year of {{jalink|Emperor Sujin|Q333530}}, the deity {{jalink|Amaterasu|Q455602}} enshrined in the {{jalink|Imperial Palace|Q500681}} was moved to the land of Sasanuhi because of reverence. Shrine tradition states: "On the 3rd day of the 9th month in Emperor Sujin's 6th year, mirrors in the form of the sun image were cast in this place (to enshrine in the palace in place of Amaterasu), and made into the divine spirit of Amaterasu; the mirror now in the inner attendants' office is that one. This shrine enshrines the prototype mirror made at that time as {{jalink|Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|Amateru Kuniteru Hikohokari-no-Mikoto}}, and this place is therefore called Kagamitsukuri." It is said that this prototype mirror became the shrine's sacred object, and that {{jalink|Ishikoridome|Q6080112|Ishikoridome-no-Mikoto}} (who forged the {{jalink|Yata no Kagami|Q2610017}} when Amaterasu hid in the {{jalink|Ame-no-Iwato|Q3231835}}) and her father {{jalink|Ame-no-Nukado}} no Mikoto were also enshrined here. The ''{{jalink|Wamyosho}}'' records "Kagamitsukuri-go" in {{jalink|Yamato Province|Q907495}}, {{jalink|Shikige District|Q11486987|Jokage District}}. Since the mirror-making clan (kagamitsukuri-be) is said to have lived here producing mirrors, the shrine's founding is thought to be closely tied to that clan. Nearby shrines such as {{jalink|Kagamitsukuri Ita Shrine|Q54153278}} and {{jalink|Kagamitsukuri Maki Shrine|Q43594607}} are also pointed out as related. [[ファイル:鏡作坐天照御魂神社 御神宝.jpg|サムネイル|右]] In {{jalink|Daido (Japan)|Q1373191|Daido}} 1 ({{jalink|806|Q30021}}), eighteen households were donated as shrine estates (''{{jalink|Shinshoku Kofu Sho|Q11502312}}''). In the ''{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932}}'' it is listed as a major shrine and noted to receive offerings for monthly rites and the {{jalink|Niiname-sai|Q11501518|new harvest rite}}. On {{jalink|January 27|Q2284}}, {{jalink|Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|Ten'an}} 3 ({{jalink|859|Q23897}}), it was granted divine rank from junior fifth lower to junior fifth upper (''{{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}'') - recorded as "Kagamitsukuri Amateru Mitama no Kami." From the medieval period onward it was called "Kagamitsukuri Sansho Daimyojin," but is said to have declined until the {{jalink|Tawaramoto Domain|Q10425374}} was established in the {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}} (then a {{jalink|kōtai yoriai|Q11373913}} holding of the {{jalink|Hirano clan|Q11483690|Hirano family}}). From the Edo period onward, it drew deep devotion from the mirror industry. After entering the {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} era and issuance of the {{jalink|Shinbutsu Bunri Edict}}, the shrine temple, the {{jalink|Shingon sect|Q550182}} Kangakurin of Iishu-zan Jingū-ji, was abolished. However, its bell tower alone remained and survives to the present. In April {{jalink|1873|Q7757}} (Meiji 6) it was ranked as a {{jalink|gōsha|Q134917316}}, and in {{jalink|1936|Q18649}} ({{jalink|Showa|Q327567}} 11) it was ranked as {{jalink|kensha}}. It possesses the sacred treasure called "three divine mirrors with two beasts." === Precinct === [[File:Kagamitsukuri-jinja, honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|Main hall]] [[File:Kagamitsukuri-jinja, Otauesai.jpg|thumb|220px|right|Rice-planting festival]] * 3 main halls ** First hall ** Second hall ** Third hall * Haiden * Kagamitsukuri-za {{jalink|Wakamiya Shrine|Q11616280}} * {{jalink|Amaterasu|Q455602}} Shrine * {{jalink|Tajikarao Shrine|Q11496923}} * {{jalink|Sumiyoshi Shrine|Q11381857}} * {{jalink|Kasuga Shrine|Q10855011}} * Gosha-sha (combined shrine) - {{jalink|Kasu嶋 Shrine}}, Hachiekanekami Shrine, {{jalink|Ukemochi|Q2280323}} Shrine, {{jalink|Itsukushima Shrine}}, {{jalink|Kotoshironushi}} Shrine, {{jalink|Sarutahiko}} Shrine, {{jalink|Ōkuninushi}} Shrine * {{jalink|Kotohira Shrine|Q11646488}} - stone monument * Sayorihime Shrine * Kagitori Shrine * {{jalink|Fuefuki Shrine}} * {{jalink|Aizome Shrine}} * Kagami Pond * Kagami Stone - unearthed from Kagami Pond. It is said to have been used to polish mirror surfaces during mirror making. * Bell tower - bell tower of Jingū-ji Kangakurin. The {{jalink|bonsho|Q8192099}} was made in {{jalink|Kansei|Q1195131}} 7 ({{jalink|1795|Q7815}}). During the {{jalink|Pacific War|Q184425}} it was surrendered under the {{jalink|metal collection order|Q11646956}}, but the war ended before it was melted and it was safely returned to the shrine. * Shrine office ファイル:Kagamitsukuri Jinja Haiden.jpg|Haiden ファイル:Kagamitsukuri Jinja Kagamiishi.jpg|Kagami Stone ファイル:Kagamitsukuri Wakamiya Jinja.jpg|Kagamitsukuri Wakamiya Shrine === Access === * 15 minutes on foot northeast from {{jalink|Tawaramoto Station|Q7689224}} on the {{jalink|Kintetsu|Q1531085|Kintetsu}} {{jalink|Kashihara Line}}. === See also === {{Commonscat}} * {{jalink|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|Q11433427}} === References === * Information board by Tawaramoto Board of Education === External links === *[http://www.town.tawaramoto.nara.jp/kanko/bunkazai/temple/3446.html Kagamitsukuri Shrine] - Tawaramoto Town *[http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22807 Kagamitsukuri Nimasu Amateru Mitama Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Compilation of Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials" {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かかみつくりにますあまてるみたましんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kagamitsukuri Nimasu Amateru Mitama Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kagamitsukuri Nimasu Amateru Mitama Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧県社]] [[Category:田原本町の建築物]] [[Category:田原本町の歴史]] [[Category:pages]]

Mitama Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kagamitsukuri Nimasu Amateru Mitama Shrine''' (かがみつくりにますあまてるみたまじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Tawaramoto|Q1203159}}, {{jalink|Shiki District|Q1155036}}, {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}. It is a {{jalink|major Shikinaisha|Q134917288}}, and its former {{jalink|shrine rank|Q10444029}} was {{jalink|kensha}}. It is said to have been founded by the Kagamitsukuri-be mirror-making clan, who lived in this area from the 4th to 5th centuries producing mirrors, and enshrined their tutelary deity ({{jalink|Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|Amateru Kuniteru Hikohokari-no-Mikoto}}) together with their ancestor ({{jalink|Ishikoridome|Q6080112|Ishikoridome-no-Mikoto}}). === Enshrined deities === The three enshrined deities are: * {{jalink|Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|Amateru Kuniteru Hikohokari-no-Mikoto}} (あまてるくにてるひこほあかりのみこと) * {{jalink|Ishikoridome|Q6080112|Ishikoridome-no-Mikoto}} (いしこりどめのみこと) * {{jalink|Ame-no-Nukado}} no Mikoto (あめのぬかどのみこと) In the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|Register of Shrines}}, only one deity seat is recorded. Because the shrine name means "the shrine of Amateru Mitama no Kami seated at Kagamitsukuri," it is considered originally a solar-cult shrine devoted to "Amateru Mitama no Kami." In the Jinmyocho, shrines called Amateru Mitama Jinja exist in several regions of {{jalink|Japan|Q17}}; these are regarded as solar deities of a different {{jalink|divine status}} from {{jalink|Amaterasu|Q455602}} (the imperial ancestral deity). === History === According to classical chronicles ({{jalink|Kiki|Q842308}}), in the 6th year of {{jalink|Emperor Sujin|Q333530}}, the deity {{jalink|Amaterasu|Q455602}} enshrined in the {{jalink|Imperial Palace|Q500681}} was moved to the land of Sasanuhi because of reverence. Shrine tradition states: "On the 3rd day of the 9th month in Emperor Sujin's 6th year, mirrors in the form of the sun image were cast in this place (to enshrine in the palace in place of Amaterasu), and made into the divine spirit of Amaterasu; the mirror now in the inner attendants' office is that one. This shrine enshrines the prototype mirror made at that time as {{jalink|Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|Amateru Kuniteru Hikohokari-no-Mikoto}}, and this place is therefore called Kagamitsukuri." It is said that this prototype mirror became the shrine's sacred object, and that {{jalink|Ishikoridome|Q6080112|Ishikoridome-no-Mikoto}} (who forged the {{jalink|Yata no Kagami|Q2610017}} when Amaterasu hid in the {{jalink|Ame-no-Iwato|Q3231835}}) and her father {{jalink|Ame-no-Nukado}} no Mikoto were also enshrined here. The ''{{jalink|Wamyosho}}'' records "Kagamitsukuri-go" in {{jalink|Yamato Province|Q907495}}, {{jalink|Shikige District|Q11486987|Jokage District}}. Since the mirror-making clan (kagamitsukuri-be) is said to have lived here producing mirrors, the shrine's founding is thought to be closely tied to that clan. Nearby shrines such as {{jalink|Kagamitsukuri Ita Shrine|Q54153278}} and {{jalink|Kagamitsukuri Maki Shrine|Q43594607}} are also pointed out as related. [[ファイル:鏡作坐天照御魂神社 御神宝.jpg|サムネイル|右]] In {{jalink|Daido (Japan)|Q1373191|Daido}} 1 ({{jalink|806|Q30021}}), eighteen households were donated as shrine estates (''{{jalink|Shinshoku Kofu Sho|Q11502312}}''). In the ''{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932}}'' it is listed as a major shrine and noted to receive offerings for monthly rites and the {{jalink|Niiname-sai|Q11501518|new harvest rite}}. On {{jalink|January 27|Q2284}}, {{jalink|Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|Ten'an}} 3 ({{jalink|859|Q23897}}), it was granted divine rank from junior fifth lower to junior fifth upper (''{{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}'') - recorded as "Kagamitsukuri Amateru Mitama no Kami." From the medieval period onward it was called "Kagamitsukuri Sansho Daimyojin," but is said to have declined until the {{jalink|Tawaramoto Domain|Q10425374}} was established in the {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}} (then a {{jalink|kōtai yoriai|Q11373913}} holding of the {{jalink|Hirano clan|Q11483690|Hirano family}}). From the Edo period onward, it drew deep devotion from the mirror industry. After entering the {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} era and issuance of the {{jalink|Shinbutsu Bunri Edict}}, the shrine temple, the {{jalink|Shingon sect|Q550182}} Kangakurin of Iishu-zan Jingū-ji, was abolished. However, its bell tower alone remained and survives to the present. In April {{jalink|1873|Q7757}} (Meiji 6) it was ranked as a {{jalink|gōsha|Q134917316}}, and in {{jalink|1936|Q18649}} ({{jalink|Showa|Q327567}} 11) it was ranked as {{jalink|kensha}}. It possesses the sacred treasure called "three divine mirrors with two beasts." === Precinct === [[File:Kagamitsukuri-jinja, honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|Main hall]] [[File:Kagamitsukuri-jinja, Otauesai.jpg|thumb|220px|right|Rice-planting festival]] * 3 main halls ** First hall ** Second hall ** Third hall * Haiden * Kagamitsukuri-za {{jalink|Wakamiya Shrine|Q11616280}} * {{jalink|Amaterasu|Q455602}} Shrine * {{jalink|Tajikarao Shrine|Q11496923}} * {{jalink|Sumiyoshi Shrine|Q11381857}} * {{jalink|Kasuga Shrine|Q10855011}} * Gosha-sha (combined shrine) - {{jalink|Kasu嶋 Shrine}}, Hachiekanekami Shrine, {{jalink|Ukemochi|Q2280323}} Shrine, {{jalink|Itsukushima Shrine}}, {{jalink|Kotoshironushi}} Shrine, {{jalink|Sarutahiko}} Shrine, {{jalink|Ōkuninushi}} Shrine * {{jalink|Kotohira Shrine|Q11646488}} - stone monument * Sayorihime Shrine * Kagitori Shrine * {{jalink|Fuefuki Shrine}} * {{jalink|Aizome Shrine}} * Kagami Pond * Kagami Stone - unearthed from Kagami Pond. It is said to have been used to polish mirror surfaces during mirror making. * Bell tower - bell tower of Jingū-ji Kangakurin. The {{jalink|bonsho|Q8192099}} was made in {{jalink|Kansei|Q1195131}} 7 ({{jalink|1795|Q7815}}). During the {{jalink|Pacific War|Q184425}} it was surrendered under the {{jalink|metal collection order|Q11646956}}, but the war ended before it was melted and it was safely returned to the shrine. * Shrine office ファイル:Kagamitsukuri Jinja Haiden.jpg|Haiden ファイル:Kagamitsukuri Jinja Kagamiishi.jpg|Kagami Stone ファイル:Kagamitsukuri Wakamiya Jinja.jpg|Kagamitsukuri Wakamiya Shrine === Access === * 15 minutes on foot northeast from {{jalink|Tawaramoto Station|Q7689224}} on the {{jalink|Kintetsu|Q1531085|Kintetsu}} {{jalink|Kashihara Line}}. === See also === {{Commonscat}} * {{jalink|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|Q11433427}} === References === * Information board by Tawaramoto Board of Education === External links === *[http://www.town.tawaramoto.nara.jp/kanko/bunkazai/temple/3446.html Kagamitsukuri Shrine] - Tawaramoto Town *[http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22807 Kagamitsukuri Nimasu Amateru Mitama Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Compilation of Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials" {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かかみつくりにますあまてるみたましんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kagamitsukuri Nimasu Amateru Mitama Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kagamitsukuri Nimasu Amateru Mitama Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧県社]] [[Category:田原本町の建築物]] [[Category:田原本町の歴史]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]]

* Kagamitsukuri Jinja Torii.jpg {{commons category|Category:Kagamitsukuri-jinja}} [[JA:鏡作坐天照御魂神社]] {{wikidata link|Q38278496}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Kagamitsukuri Nimasu Amateru Mitama Shrine |画像=[[Image:Kagamitsukuri Jinja Torii.jpg|270px]]
Torii |所在地={{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}{{jalink|Shiki District|Q1155036}}{{jalink|Tawaramoto|Q1203159}} Yao 816 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{jalink|Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|Amateru Kuniteru Hikohokari-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Ishikoridome|Q6080112|Ishikoridome-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Ame-no-Nukado}} no Mikoto |神体=Three divine mirrors with two beasts |社格={{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (major)
former {{jalink|kensha}} |創建={{jalink|Emperor Sujin|Q333530}} 6 ({{jalink|92 BCE|Q42350}}) |本殿=Three-bay shrine, {{jalink|kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781}} |別名=Kagamitsukuri Shrine |例祭={{jalink|October 25|Q2954}} |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Kagamitsukuri Nimasu Amateru
Mitama Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kagamitsukuri Nimasu Amateru Mitama Shrine''' (かがみつくりにますあまてるみたまじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Tawaramoto|Q1203159}}, {{jalink|Shiki District|Q1155036}}, {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}. It is a {{jalink|major Shikinaisha|Q134917288}}, and its former {{jalink|shrine rank|Q10444029}} was {{jalink|kensha}}. It is said to have been founded by the Kagamitsukuri-be mirror-making clan, who lived in this area from the 4th to 5th centuries producing mirrors, and enshrined their tutelary deity ({{jalink|Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|Amateru Kuniteru Hikohokari-no-Mikoto}}) together with their ancestor ({{jalink|Ishikoridome|Q6080112|Ishikoridome-no-Mikoto}}). === Enshrined deities === The three enshrined deities are: * {{jalink|Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|Amateru Kuniteru Hikohokari-no-Mikoto}} (あまてるくにてるひこほあかりのみこと) * {{jalink|Ishikoridome|Q6080112|Ishikoridome-no-Mikoto}} (いしこりどめのみこと) * {{jalink|Ame-no-Nukado}} no Mikoto (あめのぬかどのみこと) In the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|Register of Shrines}}, only one deity seat is recorded. Because the shrine name means "the shrine of Amateru Mitama no Kami seated at Kagamitsukuri," it is considered originally a solar-cult shrine devoted to "Amateru Mitama no Kami." In the Jinmyocho, shrines called Amateru Mitama Jinja exist in several regions of {{jalink|Japan|Q17}}; these are regarded as solar deities of a different {{jalink|divine status}} from {{jalink|Amaterasu|Q455602}} (the imperial ancestral deity). === History === According to classical chronicles ({{jalink|Kiki|Q842308}}), in the 6th year of {{jalink|Emperor Sujin|Q333530}}, the deity {{jalink|Amaterasu|Q455602}} enshrined in the {{jalink|Imperial Palace|Q500681}} was moved to the land of Sasanuhi because of reverence. Shrine tradition states: "On the 3rd day of the 9th month in Emperor Sujin's 6th year, mirrors in the form of the sun image were cast in this place (to enshrine in the palace in place of Amaterasu), and made into the divine spirit of Amaterasu; the mirror now in the inner attendants' office is that one. This shrine enshrines the prototype mirror made at that time as {{jalink|Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|Amateru Kuniteru Hikohokari-no-Mikoto}}, and this place is therefore called Kagamitsukuri." It is said that this prototype mirror became the shrine's sacred object, and that {{jalink|Ishikoridome|Q6080112|Ishikoridome-no-Mikoto}} (who forged the {{jalink|Yata no Kagami|Q2610017}} when Amaterasu hid in the {{jalink|Ame-no-Iwato|Q3231835}}) and her father {{jalink|Ame-no-Nukado}} no Mikoto were also enshrined here. The ''{{jalink|Wamyosho}}'' records "Kagamitsukuri-go" in {{jalink|Yamato Province|Q907495}}, {{jalink|Shikige District|Q11486987|Jokage District}}. Since the mirror-making clan (kagamitsukuri-be) is said to have lived here producing mirrors, the shrine's founding is thought to be closely tied to that clan. Nearby shrines such as {{jalink|Kagamitsukuri Ita Shrine|Q54153278}} and {{jalink|Kagamitsukuri Maki Shrine|Q43594607}} are also pointed out as related. [[ファイル:鏡作坐天照御魂神社 御神宝.jpg|サムネイル|右]] In {{jalink|Daido (Japan)|Q1373191|Daido}} 1 ({{jalink|806|Q30021}}), eighteen households were donated as shrine estates (''{{jalink|Shinshoku Kofu Sho|Q11502312}}''). In the ''{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932}}'' it is listed as a major shrine and noted to receive offerings for monthly rites and the {{jalink|Niiname-sai|Q11501518|new harvest rite}}. On {{jalink|January 27|Q2284}}, {{jalink|Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|Ten'an}} 3 ({{jalink|859|Q23897}}), it was granted divine rank from junior fifth lower to junior fifth upper (''{{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}'') - recorded as "Kagamitsukuri Amateru Mitama no Kami." From the medieval period onward it was called "Kagamitsukuri Sansho Daimyojin," but is said to have declined until the {{jalink|Tawaramoto Domain|Q10425374}} was established in the {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}} (then a {{jalink|kōtai yoriai|Q11373913}} holding of the {{jalink|Hirano clan|Q11483690|Hirano family}}). From the Edo period onward, it drew deep devotion from the mirror industry. After entering the {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} era and issuance of the {{jalink|Shinbutsu Bunri Edict}}, the shrine temple, the {{jalink|Shingon sect|Q550182}} Kangakurin of Iishu-zan Jingū-ji, was abolished. However, its bell tower alone remained and survives to the present. In April {{jalink|1873|Q7757}} (Meiji 6) it was ranked as a {{jalink|gōsha|Q134917316}}, and in {{jalink|1936|Q18649}} ({{jalink|Showa|Q327567}} 11) it was ranked as {{jalink|kensha}}. It possesses the sacred treasure called "three divine mirrors with two beasts." === Precinct === [[File:Kagamitsukuri-jinja, honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|Main hall]] [[File:Kagamitsukuri-jinja, Otauesai.jpg|thumb|220px|right|Rice-planting festival]] * 3 main halls ** First hall ** Second hall ** Third hall * Haiden * Kagamitsukuri-za {{jalink|Wakamiya Shrine|Q11616280}} * {{jalink|Amaterasu|Q455602}} Shrine * {{jalink|Tajikarao Shrine|Q11496923}} * {{jalink|Sumiyoshi Shrine|Q11381857}} * {{jalink|Kasuga Shrine|Q10855011}} * Gosha-sha (combined shrine) - {{jalink|Kasu嶋 Shrine}}, Hachiekanekami Shrine, {{jalink|Ukemochi|Q2280323}} Shrine, {{jalink|Itsukushima Shrine}}, {{jalink|Kotoshironushi}} Shrine, {{jalink|Sarutahiko}} Shrine, {{jalink|Ōkuninushi}} Shrine * {{jalink|Kotohira Shrine|Q11646488}} - stone monument * Sayorihime Shrine * Kagitori Shrine * {{jalink|Fuefuki Shrine}} * {{jalink|Aizome Shrine}} * Kagami Pond * Kagami Stone - unearthed from Kagami Pond. It is said to have been used to polish mirror surfaces during mirror making. * Bell tower - bell tower of Jingū-ji Kangakurin. The {{jalink|bonsho|Q8192099}} was made in {{jalink|Kansei|Q1195131}} 7 ({{jalink|1795|Q7815}}). During the {{jalink|Pacific War|Q184425}} it was surrendered under the {{jalink|metal collection order|Q11646956}}, but the war ended before it was melted and it was safely returned to the shrine. * Shrine office ファイル:Kagamitsukuri Jinja Haiden.jpg|Haiden ファイル:Kagamitsukuri Jinja Kagamiishi.jpg|Kagami Stone ファイル:Kagamitsukuri Wakamiya Jinja.jpg|Kagamitsukuri Wakamiya Shrine === Access === * 15 minutes on foot northeast from {{jalink|Tawaramoto Station|Q7689224}} on the {{jalink|Kintetsu|Q1531085|Kintetsu}} {{jalink|Kashihara Line}}. === See also === {{Commonscat}} * {{jalink|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|Q11433427}} === References === * Information board by Tawaramoto Board of Education === External links === *[http://www.town.tawaramoto.nara.jp/kanko/bunkazai/temple/3446.html Kagamitsukuri Shrine] - Tawaramoto Town *[http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22807 Kagamitsukuri Nimasu Amateru Mitama Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Compilation of Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials" {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かかみつくりにますあまてるみたましんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kagamitsukuri Nimasu Amateru Mitama Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kagamitsukuri Nimasu Amateru Mitama Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧県社]] [[Category:田原本町の建築物]] [[Category:田原本町の歴史]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

* Kagamitsukuri Jinja Torii.jpg {{commons category|Category:Kagamitsukuri-jinja}} [[JA:鏡作坐天照御魂神社]] {{wikidata link|Q38278496}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Kagamitsukuri Nimasu Amateru Mitama Shrine |画像=[[Image:Kagamitsukuri Jinja Torii.jpg|270px]]
Torii |所在地={{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}{{jalink|Shiki District|Q1155036}}{{jalink|Tawaramoto|Q1203159}} Yao 816 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{jalink|Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|Amateru Kuniteru Hikohokari-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Ishikoridome|Q6080112|Ishikoridome-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Ame-no-Nukado}} no Mikoto |神体=Three divine mirrors with two beasts |社格={{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (major)
former {{jalink|kensha}} |創建={{jalink|Emperor Sujin|Q333530}} 6 ({{jalink|92 BCE|Q42350}}) |本殿=Three-bay shrine, {{jalink|kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781}} |別名=Kagamitsukuri Shrine |例祭={{jalink|October 25|Q2954}} |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Kagamitsukuri Nimasu Amateru
Mitama Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kagamitsukuri Nimasu Amateru Mitama Shrine''' (かがみつくりにますあまてるみたまじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Tawaramoto|Q1203159}}, {{jalink|Shiki District|Q1155036}}, {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}. It is a {{jalink|major Shikinaisha|Q134917288}}, and its former {{jalink|shrine rank|Q10444029}} was {{jalink|kensha}}. It is said to have been founded by the Kagamitsukuri-be mirror-making clan, who lived in this area from the 4th to 5th centuries producing mirrors, and enshrined their tutelary deity ({{jalink|Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|Amateru Kuniteru Hikohokari-no-Mikoto}}) together with their ancestor ({{jalink|Ishikoridome|Q6080112|Ishikoridome-no-Mikoto}}). === Enshrined deities === The three enshrined deities are: * {{jalink|Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|Amateru Kuniteru Hikohokari-no-Mikoto}} (あまてるくにてるひこほあかりのみこと) * {{jalink|Ishikoridome|Q6080112|Ishikoridome-no-Mikoto}} (いしこりどめのみこと) * {{jalink|Ame-no-Nukado}} no Mikoto (あめのぬかどのみこと) In the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|Register of Shrines}}, only one deity seat is recorded. Because the shrine name means "the shrine of Amateru Mitama no Kami seated at Kagamitsukuri," it is considered originally a solar-cult shrine devoted to "Amateru Mitama no Kami." In the Jinmyocho, shrines called Amateru Mitama Jinja exist in several regions of {{jalink|Japan|Q17}}; these are regarded as solar deities of a different {{jalink|divine status}} from {{jalink|Amaterasu|Q455602}} (the imperial ancestral deity). === History === According to classical chronicles ({{jalink|Kiki|Q842308}}), in the 6th year of {{jalink|Emperor Sujin|Q333530}}, the deity {{jalink|Amaterasu|Q455602}} enshrined in the {{jalink|Imperial Palace|Q500681}} was moved to the land of Sasanuhi because of reverence. Shrine tradition states: "On the 3rd day of the 9th month in Emperor Sujin's 6th year, mirrors in the form of the sun image were cast in this place (to enshrine in the palace in place of Amaterasu), and made into the divine spirit of Amaterasu; the mirror now in the inner attendants' office is that one. This shrine enshrines the prototype mirror made at that time as {{jalink|Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|Amateru Kuniteru Hikohokari-no-Mikoto}}, and this place is therefore called Kagamitsukuri." It is said that this prototype mirror became the shrine's sacred object, and that {{jalink|Ishikoridome|Q6080112|Ishikoridome-no-Mikoto}} (who forged the {{jalink|Yata no Kagami|Q2610017}} when Amaterasu hid in the {{jalink|Ame-no-Iwato|Q3231835}}) and her father {{jalink|Ame-no-Nukado}} no Mikoto were also enshrined here. The ''{{jalink|Wamyosho}}'' records "Kagamitsukuri-go" in {{jalink|Yamato Province|Q907495}}, {{jalink|Shikige District|Q11486987|Jokage District}}. Since the mirror-making clan (kagamitsukuri-be) is said to have lived here producing mirrors, the shrine's founding is thought to be closely tied to that clan. Nearby shrines such as {{jalink|Kagamitsukuri Ita Shrine|Q54153278}} and {{jalink|Kagamitsukuri Maki Shrine|Q43594607}} are also pointed out as related. [[ファイル:鏡作坐天照御魂神社 御神宝.jpg|サムネイル|右]] In {{jalink|Daido (Japan)|Q1373191|Daido}} 1 ({{jalink|806|Q30021}}), eighteen households were donated as shrine estates (''{{jalink|Shinshoku Kofu Sho|Q11502312}}''). In the ''{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932}}'' it is listed as a major shrine and noted to receive offerings for monthly rites and the {{jalink|Niiname-sai|Q11501518|new harvest rite}}. On {{jalink|January 27|Q2284}}, {{jalink|Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|Ten'an}} 3 ({{jalink|859|Q23897}}), it was granted divine rank from junior fifth lower to junior fifth upper (''{{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}'') - recorded as "Kagamitsukuri Amateru Mitama no Kami." From the medieval period onward it was called "Kagamitsukuri Sansho Daimyojin," but is said to have declined until the {{jalink|Tawaramoto Domain|Q10425374}} was established in the {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}} (then a {{jalink|kōtai yoriai|Q11373913}} holding of the {{jalink|Hirano clan|Q11483690|Hirano family}}). From the Edo period onward, it drew deep devotion from the mirror industry. After entering the {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} era and issuance of the {{jalink|Shinbutsu Bunri Edict}}, the shrine temple, the {{jalink|Shingon sect|Q550182}} Kangakurin of Iishu-zan Jingū-ji, was abolished. However, its bell tower alone remained and survives to the present. In April {{jalink|1873|Q7757}} (Meiji 6) it was ranked as a {{jalink|gōsha|Q134917316}}, and in {{jalink|1936|Q18649}} ({{jalink|Showa|Q327567}} 11) it was ranked as {{jalink|kensha}}. It possesses the sacred treasure called "three divine mirrors with two beasts." === Precinct === [[File:Kagamitsukuri-jinja, honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|Main hall]] [[File:Kagamitsukuri-jinja, Otauesai.jpg|thumb|220px|right|Rice-planting festival]] * 3 main halls ** First hall ** Second hall ** Third hall * Haiden * Kagamitsukuri-za {{jalink|Wakamiya Shrine|Q11616280}} * {{jalink|Amaterasu|Q455602}} Shrine * {{jalink|Tajikarao Shrine|Q11496923}} * {{jalink|Sumiyoshi Shrine|Q11381857}} * {{jalink|Kasuga Shrine|Q10855011}} * Gosha-sha (combined shrine) - {{jalink|Kasu嶋 Shrine}}, Hachiekanekami Shrine, {{jalink|Ukemochi|Q2280323}} Shrine, {{jalink|Itsukushima Shrine}}, {{jalink|Kotoshironushi}} Shrine, {{jalink|Sarutahiko}} Shrine, {{jalink|Ōkuninushi}} Shrine * {{jalink|Kotohira Shrine|Q11646488}} - stone monument * Sayorihime Shrine * Kagitori Shrine * {{jalink|Fuefuki Shrine}} * {{jalink|Aizome Shrine}} * Kagami Pond * Kagami Stone - unearthed from Kagami Pond. It is said to have been used to polish mirror surfaces during mirror making. * Bell tower - bell tower of Jingū-ji Kangakurin. The {{jalink|bonsho|Q8192099}} was made in {{jalink|Kansei|Q1195131}} 7 ({{jalink|1795|Q7815}}). During the {{jalink|Pacific War|Q184425}} it was surrendered under the {{jalink|metal collection order|Q11646956}}, but the war ended before it was melted and it was safely returned to the shrine. * Shrine office ファイル:Kagamitsukuri Jinja Haiden.jpg|Haiden ファイル:Kagamitsukuri Jinja Kagamiishi.jpg|Kagami Stone ファイル:Kagamitsukuri Wakamiya Jinja.jpg|Kagamitsukuri Wakamiya Shrine === Access === * 15 minutes on foot northeast from {{jalink|Tawaramoto Station|Q7689224}} on the {{jalink|Kintetsu|Q1531085|Kintetsu}} {{jalink|Kashihara Line}}. === See also === {{Commonscat}} * {{jalink|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|Q11433427}} === References === * Information board by Tawaramoto Board of Education === External links === *[http://www.town.tawaramoto.nara.jp/kanko/bunkazai/temple/3446.html Kagamitsukuri Shrine] - Tawaramoto Town *[http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22807 Kagamitsukuri Nimasu Amateru Mitama Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Compilation of Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials" {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かかみつくりにますあまてるみたましんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kagamitsukuri Nimasu Amateru Mitama Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kagamitsukuri Nimasu Amateru Mitama Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧県社]] [[Category:田原本町の建築物]] [[Category:田原本町の歴史]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

* Kagamitsukuri Jinja Torii.jpg {{commons category|Category:Kagamitsukuri-jinja}} [[JA:鏡作坐天照御魂神社]] {{wikidata link|Q38278496}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Kagamitsukuri Nimasu Amateru Mitama Shrine |画像=[[Image:Kagamitsukuri Jinja Torii.jpg|270px]]
Torii |所在地={{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}{{ill|lt=Shiki District|Q1155036|WD=Q1155036}}{{ill|lt=Tawaramoto|Q1203159|WD=Q1203159}} Yao 816 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411|lt=Amateru Kuniteru Hikohokari-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Ishikoridome|Q6080112|WD=Q6080112|lt=Ishikoridome-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill||lt=Ame-no-Nukado|UNKNOWN}} no Mikoto |神体=Three divine mirrors with two beasts |社格={{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} (major)
former {{ill||lt=kensha|UNKNOWN}} |創建={{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530}} 6 ({{ill|lt=92 BCE|Q42350|WD=Q42350}}) |本殿=Three-bay shrine, {{ill|lt=kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781}} |別名=Kagamitsukuri Shrine |例祭={{ill|lt=October 25|Q2954|WD=Q2954}} |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Kagamitsukuri Nimasu Amateru
Mitama Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kagamitsukuri Nimasu Amateru Mitama Shrine''' (かがみつくりにますあまてるみたまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Tawaramoto|Q1203159|WD=Q1203159}}, {{ill|lt=Shiki District|Q1155036|WD=Q1155036}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}. It is a {{ill|lt=major Shikinaisha|Q134917288|WD=Q134917288}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} was {{ill||lt=kensha|UNKNOWN}}. It is said to have been founded by the Kagamitsukuri-be mirror-making clan, who lived in this area from the 4th to 5th centuries producing mirrors, and enshrined their tutelary deity ({{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411|lt=Amateru Kuniteru Hikohokari-no-Mikoto}}) together with their ancestor ({{ill|lt=Ishikoridome|Q6080112|WD=Q6080112|lt=Ishikoridome-no-Mikoto}}). === Enshrined deities === The three enshrined deities are: * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411|lt=Amateru Kuniteru Hikohokari-no-Mikoto}} (あまてるくにてるひこほあかりのみこと) * {{ill|lt=Ishikoridome|Q6080112|WD=Q6080112|lt=Ishikoridome-no-Mikoto}} (いしこりどめのみこと) * {{ill||lt=Ame-no-Nukado|UNKNOWN}} no Mikoto (あめのぬかどのみこと) In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Register of Shrines}}, only one deity seat is recorded. Because the shrine name means "the shrine of Amateru Mitama no Kami seated at Kagamitsukuri," it is considered originally a solar-cult shrine devoted to "Amateru Mitama no Kami." In the Jinmyocho, shrines called Amateru Mitama Jinja exist in several regions of {{ill|lt=Japan|Q17|WD=Q17}}; these are regarded as solar deities of a different {{ill||lt=divine status|UNKNOWN}} from {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602}} (the imperial ancestral deity). === History === According to classical chronicles ({{ill|lt=Kiki|Q842308|WD=Q842308}}), in the 6th year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530}}, the deity {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602}} enshrined in the {{ill|lt=Imperial Palace|Q500681|WD=Q500681}} was moved to the land of Sasanuhi because of reverence. Shrine tradition states: "On the 3rd day of the 9th month in Emperor Sujin's 6th year, mirrors in the form of the sun image were cast in this place (to enshrine in the palace in place of Amaterasu), and made into the divine spirit of Amaterasu; the mirror now in the inner attendants' office is that one. This shrine enshrines the prototype mirror made at that time as {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411|lt=Amateru Kuniteru Hikohokari-no-Mikoto}}, and this place is therefore called Kagamitsukuri." It is said that this prototype mirror became the shrine's sacred object, and that {{ill|lt=Ishikoridome|Q6080112|WD=Q6080112|lt=Ishikoridome-no-Mikoto}} (who forged the {{ill|lt=Yata no Kagami|Q2610017|WD=Q2610017}} when Amaterasu hid in the {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Iwato|Q3231835|WD=Q3231835}}) and her father {{ill||lt=Ame-no-Nukado|UNKNOWN}} no Mikoto were also enshrined here. The ''{{ill||lt=Wamyosho|UNKNOWN}}'' records "Kagamitsukuri-go" in {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495}}, {{ill|lt=Shikige District|Q11486987|WD=Q11486987|lt=Jokage District}}. Since the mirror-making clan (kagamitsukuri-be) is said to have lived here producing mirrors, the shrine's founding is thought to be closely tied to that clan. Nearby shrines such as {{ill|lt=Kagamitsukuri Ita Shrine|Q54153278|WD=Q54153278}} and {{ill|lt=Kagamitsukuri Maki Shrine|Q43594607|WD=Q43594607}} are also pointed out as related. [[ファイル:鏡作坐天照御魂神社 御神宝.jpg|サムネイル|右]] In {{ill|lt=Daido (Japan)|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191|lt=Daido}} 1 ({{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021}}), eighteen households were donated as shrine estates (''{{ill|lt=Shinshoku Kofu Sho|Q11502312|WD=Q11502312}}''). In the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}'' it is listed as a major shrine and noted to receive offerings for monthly rites and the {{ill|lt=Niiname-sai|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518|lt=new harvest rite}}. On {{ill|lt=January 27|Q2284|WD=Q2284}}, {{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897}}), it was granted divine rank from junior fifth lower to junior fifth upper (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}'') - recorded as "Kagamitsukuri Amateru Mitama no Kami." From the medieval period onward it was called "Kagamitsukuri Sansho Daimyojin," but is said to have declined until the {{ill|lt=Tawaramoto Domain|Q10425374|WD=Q10425374}} was established in the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}} (then a {{ill|lt=kōtai yoriai|Q11373913|WD=Q11373913}} holding of the {{ill|lt=Hirano clan|Q11483690|WD=Q11483690|lt=Hirano family}}). From the Edo period onward, it drew deep devotion from the mirror industry. After entering the {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} era and issuance of the {{ill||lt=Shinbutsu Bunri Edict|UNKNOWN}}, the shrine temple, the {{ill|lt=Shingon sect|Q550182|WD=Q550182}} Kangakurin of Iishu-zan Jingū-ji, was abolished. However, its bell tower alone remained and survives to the present. In April {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757}} (Meiji 6) it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}, and in {{ill|lt=1936|Q18649|WD=Q18649}} ({{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 11) it was ranked as {{ill||lt=kensha|UNKNOWN}}. It possesses the sacred treasure called "three divine mirrors with two beasts." === Precinct === [[File:Kagamitsukuri-jinja, honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|Main hall]] [[File:Kagamitsukuri-jinja, Otauesai.jpg|thumb|220px|right|Rice-planting festival]] * 3 main halls ** First hall ** Second hall ** Third hall * Haiden * Kagamitsukuri-za {{ill|lt=Wakamiya Shrine|Q11616280|WD=Q11616280}} * {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602}} Shrine * {{ill|lt=Tajikarao Shrine|Q11496923|WD=Q11496923}} * {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Shrine|Q11381857|WD=Q11381857}} * {{ill|lt=Kasuga Shrine|Q10855011|WD=Q10855011}} * Gosha-sha (combined shrine) - {{ill||lt=Kasu嶋 Shrine|UNKNOWN}}, Hachiekanekami Shrine, {{ill|lt=Ukemochi|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323}} Shrine, {{ill||lt=Itsukushima Shrine|UNKNOWN}}, {{ill||lt=Kotoshironushi|UNKNOWN}} Shrine, {{ill||lt=Sarutahiko|UNKNOWN}} Shrine, {{ill||lt=Ōkuninushi|UNKNOWN}} Shrine * {{ill|lt=Kotohira Shrine|Q11646488|WD=Q11646488}} - stone monument * Sayorihime Shrine * Kagitori Shrine * {{ill||lt=Fuefuki Shrine|UNKNOWN}} * {{ill||lt=Aizome Shrine|UNKNOWN}} * Kagami Pond * Kagami Stone - unearthed from Kagami Pond. It is said to have been used to polish mirror surfaces during mirror making. * Bell tower - bell tower of Jingū-ji Kangakurin. The {{ill|lt=bonsho|Q8192099|WD=Q8192099}} was made in {{ill|lt=Kansei|Q1195131|WD=Q1195131}} 7 ({{ill|lt=1795|Q7815|WD=Q7815}}). During the {{ill|lt=Pacific War|Q184425|WD=Q184425}} it was surrendered under the {{ill|lt=metal collection order|Q11646956|WD=Q11646956}}, but the war ended before it was melted and it was safely returned to the shrine. * Shrine office ファイル:Kagamitsukuri Jinja Haiden.jpg|Haiden ファイル:Kagamitsukuri Jinja Kagamiishi.jpg|Kagami Stone ファイル:Kagamitsukuri Wakamiya Jinja.jpg|Kagamitsukuri Wakamiya Shrine === Access === * 15 minutes on foot northeast from {{ill|lt=Tawaramoto Station|Q7689224|WD=Q7689224}} on the {{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu}} {{ill||lt=Kashihara Line|UNKNOWN}}. === See also === {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|Q11433427|WD=Q11433427}} === References === * Information board by Tawaramoto Board of Education === External links === *[http://www.town.tawaramoto.nara.jp/kanko/bunkazai/temple/3446.html Kagamitsukuri Shrine] - Tawaramoto Town *[http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22807 Kagamitsukuri Nimasu Amateru Mitama Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Compilation of Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials" {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かかみつくりにますあまてるみたましんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kagamitsukuri Nimasu Amateru Mitama Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kagamitsukuri Nimasu Amateru Mitama Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧県社]] [[Category:田原本町の建築物]] [[Category:田原本町の歴史]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]
