Torii |所在地={{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Shiki District|Q1155036|WD=Q1155036|1=Shiki District, Nara}}{{ill|lt=Tawaramoto|Q1203159|WD=Q1203159|1=Tawaramoto, Nara}} Yao 816 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411|lt=Amateru Kuniteru Hikohokari-no-Mikoto|1=Amenohoakari}}
{{ill|lt=Ishikoridome|Q6080112|WD=Q6080112|lt=Ishikoridome-no-Mikoto|1=Ishikori-dome no Mikoto}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Nukado|1=UNKNOWN}} no Mikoto |神体=Three divine mirrors with two beasts |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (major)
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} |創建={{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}} 6 ({{ill|lt=92 BCE|Q42350|WD=Q42350|1=92 BC}}) |本殿=Three-bay shrine, {{ill|lt=kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} |別名=Kagamitsukuri Shrine |例祭={{ill|lt=October 25|Q2954|WD=Q2954|1=October 25}} |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Kagamitsukuri Nimasu Amateru
Mitama Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kagamitsukuri Nimasu Amateru Mitama Shrine''' (かがみつくりにますあまてるみたまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Tawaramoto|Q1203159|WD=Q1203159|1=Tawaramoto, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Shiki District|Q1155036|WD=Q1155036|1=Shiki District, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=major Shikinaisha|Q134917288|WD=Q134917288|1=Shikinai Taisha}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. It is said to have been founded by the Kagamitsukuri-be mirror-making clan, who lived in this area from the 4th to 5th centuries producing mirrors, and enshrined their tutelary deity ({{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411|lt=Amateru Kuniteru Hikohokari-no-Mikoto|1=Amenohoakari}}) together with their ancestor ({{ill|lt=Ishikoridome|Q6080112|WD=Q6080112|lt=Ishikoridome-no-Mikoto|1=Ishikori-dome no Mikoto}}). === Enshrined deities === The three enshrined deities are: * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411|lt=Amateru Kuniteru Hikohokari-no-Mikoto|1=Amenohoakari}} (あまてるくにてるひこほあかりのみこと) * {{ill|lt=Ishikoridome|Q6080112|WD=Q6080112|lt=Ishikoridome-no-Mikoto|1=Ishikori-dome no Mikoto}} (いしこりどめのみこと) * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Nukado|1=UNKNOWN}} no Mikoto (あめのぬかどのみこと) In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Register of Shrines|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, only one deity seat is recorded. Because the shrine name means "the shrine of Amateru Mitama no Kami seated at Kagamitsukuri," it is considered originally a solar-cult shrine devoted to "Amateru Mitama no Kami." In the Jinmyocho, shrines called Amateru Mitama Jinja exist in several regions of {{ill|lt=Japan|Q17|WD=Q17|1=Japan}}; these are regarded as solar deities of a different {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=divine status|1=UNKNOWN}} from {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} (the imperial ancestral deity). === History === According to classical chronicles ({{ill|lt=Kiki|Q842308|WD=Q842308|1=Kiki (Shinto)}}), in the 6th year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}}, the deity {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} enshrined in the {{ill|lt=Imperial Palace|Q500681|WD=Q500681|1=Tokyo Imperial Palace}} was moved to the land of Sasanuhi because of reverence. Shrine tradition states: "On the 3rd day of the 9th month in Emperor Sujin's 6th year, mirrors in the form of the sun image were cast in this place (to enshrine in the palace in place of Amaterasu), and made into the divine spirit of Amaterasu; the mirror now in the inner attendants' office is that one. This shrine enshrines the prototype mirror made at that time as {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411|lt=Amateru Kuniteru Hikohokari-no-Mikoto|1=Amenohoakari}}, and this place is therefore called Kagamitsukuri." It is said that this prototype mirror became the shrine's sacred object, and that {{ill|lt=Ishikoridome|Q6080112|WD=Q6080112|lt=Ishikoridome-no-Mikoto|1=Ishikori-dome no Mikoto}} (who forged the {{ill|lt=Yata no Kagami|Q2610017|WD=Q2610017|1=Yata no Kagami}} when Amaterasu hid in the {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Iwato|Q3231835|WD=Q3231835|1=Amano-Iwato}}) and her father {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Nukado|1=UNKNOWN}} no Mikoto were also enshrined here. The ''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Wamyosho|1=UNKNOWN}}'' records "Kagamitsukuri-go" in {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}, {{ill|lt=Shikige District|Q11486987|WD=Q11486987|lt=Jokage District|1=Shikige district}}. Since the mirror-making clan (kagamitsukuri-be) is said to have lived here producing mirrors, the shrine's founding is thought to be closely tied to that clan. Nearby shrines such as {{ill|lt=Kagamitsukuri Ita Shrine|Q54153278|WD=Q54153278|1=Kagamitsukuri Ita Shrine}} and {{ill|lt=Kagamitsukuri Maki Shrine|Q43594607|WD=Q43594607|1=Kagamitsukurimake Shrine}} are also pointed out as related. [[ファイル:鏡作坐天照御魂神社 御神宝.jpg|サムネイル|右]] In {{ill|lt=Daido (Japan)|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191|lt=Daido|1=Daidō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021|1=806}}), eighteen households were donated as shrine estates (''{{ill|lt=Shinshoku Kofu Sho|Q11502312|WD=Q11502312|1=Shinshō Kyakuchokufushō}}''). In the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'' it is listed as a major shrine and noted to receive offerings for monthly rites and the {{ill|lt=Niiname-sai|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518|lt=new harvest rite|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}}. On {{ill|lt=January 27|Q2284|WD=Q2284|1=January 27}}, {{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an|1=Ten'an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}), it was granted divine rank from junior fifth lower to junior fifth upper (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'') - recorded as "Kagamitsukuri Amateru Mitama no Kami." From the medieval period onward it was called "Kagamitsukuri Sansho Daimyojin," but is said to have declined until the {{ill|lt=Tawaramoto Domain|Q10425374|WD=Q10425374|1=Tawaramoto Domain}} was established in the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}} (then a {{ill|lt=kōtai yoriai|Q11373913|WD=Q11373913|1=kōtai yoriai}} holding of the {{ill|lt=Hirano clan|Q11483690|WD=Q11483690|lt=Hirano family|1=Hirano family}}). From the Edo period onward, it drew deep devotion from the mirror industry. After entering the {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} era and issuance of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinbutsu Bunri Edict|1=UNKNOWN}}, the shrine temple, the {{ill|lt=Shingon sect|Q550182|WD=Q550182|1=Shingon Buddhism}} Kangakurin of Iishu-zan Jingū-ji, was abolished. However, its bell tower alone remained and survives to the present. In April {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} (Meiji 6) it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}, and in {{ill|lt=1936|Q18649|WD=Q18649|1=1936}} ({{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 11) it was ranked as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. It possesses the sacred treasure called "three divine mirrors with two beasts." === Precinct === [[File:Kagamitsukuri-jinja, honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|Main hall]] [[File:Kagamitsukuri-jinja, Otauesai.jpg|thumb|220px|right|Rice-planting festival]] * 3 main halls ** First hall ** Second hall ** Third hall * Haiden * Kagamitsukuri-za {{ill|lt=Wakamiya Shrine|Q11616280|WD=Q11616280|1=Wakamiya Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} Shrine * {{ill|lt=Tajikarao Shrine|Q11496923|WD=Q11496923|1=Tajikarao Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Shrine|Q11381857|WD=Q11381857|1=Sumiyoshi Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Kasuga Shrine|Q10855011|WD=Q10855011|1=Kasuga Shrines}} * Gosha-sha (combined shrine) - {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kasu嶋 Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}, Hachiekanekami Shrine, {{ill|lt=Ukemochi|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323|1=Ukemochi}} Shrine, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Itsukushima Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} Shrine, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sarutahiko|1=UNKNOWN}} Shrine, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōkuninushi|1=UNKNOWN}} Shrine * {{ill|lt=Kotohira Shrine|Q11646488|WD=Q11646488|1=Kotohira shrines}} - stone monument * Sayorihime Shrine * Kagitori Shrine * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Fuefuki Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Aizome Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} * Kagami Pond * Kagami Stone - unearthed from Kagami Pond. It is said to have been used to polish mirror surfaces during mirror making. * Bell tower - bell tower of Jingū-ji Kangakurin. The {{ill|lt=bonsho|Q8192099|WD=Q8192099|1=Bonshō}} was made in {{ill|lt=Kansei|Q1195131|WD=Q1195131|1=Kansei}} 7 ({{ill|lt=1795|Q7815|WD=Q7815|1=1795}}). During the {{ill|lt=Pacific War|Q184425|WD=Q184425|1=Pacific War}} it was surrendered under the {{ill|lt=metal collection order|Q11646956|WD=Q11646956|1=Metal Collection Order}}, but the war ended before it was melted and it was safely returned to the shrine. * Shrine office ファイル:Kagamitsukuri Jinja Haiden.jpg|Haiden ファイル:Kagamitsukuri Jinja Kagamiishi.jpg|Kagami Stone ファイル:Kagamitsukuri Wakamiya Jinja.jpg|Kagamitsukuri Wakamiya Shrine === Access === * 15 minutes on foot northeast from {{ill|lt=Tawaramoto Station|Q7689224|WD=Q7689224|1=Tawaramoto Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu|1=Kintetsu Railway}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kashihara Line|1=UNKNOWN}}. === See also === {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|Q11433427|WD=Q11433427|1=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province}} === References === * Information board by Tawaramoto Board of Education === External links === *[http://www.town.tawaramoto.nara.jp/kanko/bunkazai/temple/3446.html Kagamitsukuri Shrine] - Tawaramoto Town *[http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22807 Kagamitsukuri Nimasu Amateru Mitama Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Compilation of Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials" {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かかみつくりにますあまてるみたましんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kagamitsukuri Nimasu Amateru Mitama Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kagamitsukuri Nimasu Amateru Mitama Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧県社]] [[Category:田原本町の建築物]] [[Category:田原本町の歴史]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

Amateru Mitama Shrine |画像=[[Image:Kagamitsukuri Jinja Torii.jpg|270px]]
Torii |所在地={{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Shiki District|Q1155036|WD=Q1155036|1=Shiki District, Nara}}{{ill|lt=Tawaramoto|Q1203159|WD=Q1203159|1=Tawaramoto, Nara}} Yao 816 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411|lt=Amateru Kuniteru Hikohokari-no-Mikoto|1=Amenohoakari}}
{{ill|lt=Ishikoridome|Q6080112|WD=Q6080112|lt=Ishikoridome-no-Mikoto|1=Ishikori-dome no Mikoto}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Nukado|1=UNKNOWN}} no Mikoto |神体=Three divine mirrors with two beasts |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (major)
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} |創建={{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}} 6 ({{ill|lt=92 BCE|Q42350|WD=Q42350|1=92 BC}}) |本殿=Three-bay shrine, {{ill|lt=kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} |別名=Kagamitsukuri Shrine |例祭={{ill|lt=October 25|Q2954|WD=Q2954|1=October 25}} |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Kagamitsukuri Nimasu Amateru
Mitama Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kagamitsukuri Nimasu Amateru Mitama Shrine''' (かがみつくりにますあまてるみたまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Tawaramoto|Q1203159|WD=Q1203159|1=Tawaramoto, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Shiki District|Q1155036|WD=Q1155036|1=Shiki District, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=major Shikinaisha|Q134917288|WD=Q134917288|1=Shikinai Taisha}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. It is said to have been founded by the Kagamitsukuri-be mirror-making clan, who lived in this area from the 4th to 5th centuries producing mirrors, and enshrined their tutelary deity ({{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411|lt=Amateru Kuniteru Hikohokari-no-Mikoto|1=Amenohoakari}}) together with their ancestor ({{ill|lt=Ishikoridome|Q6080112|WD=Q6080112|lt=Ishikoridome-no-Mikoto|1=Ishikori-dome no Mikoto}}). === Enshrined deities === The three enshrined deities are: * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411|lt=Amateru Kuniteru Hikohokari-no-Mikoto|1=Amenohoakari}} (あまてるくにてるひこほあかりのみこと) * {{ill|lt=Ishikoridome|Q6080112|WD=Q6080112|lt=Ishikoridome-no-Mikoto|1=Ishikori-dome no Mikoto}} (いしこりどめのみこと) * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Nukado|1=UNKNOWN}} no Mikoto (あめのぬかどのみこと) In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Register of Shrines|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, only one deity seat is recorded. Because the shrine name means "the shrine of Amateru Mitama no Kami seated at Kagamitsukuri," it is considered originally a solar-cult shrine devoted to "Amateru Mitama no Kami." In the Jinmyocho, shrines called Amateru Mitama Jinja exist in several regions of {{ill|lt=Japan|Q17|WD=Q17|1=Japan}}; these are regarded as solar deities of a different {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=divine status|1=UNKNOWN}} from {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} (the imperial ancestral deity). === History === According to classical chronicles ({{ill|lt=Kiki|Q842308|WD=Q842308|1=Kiki (Shinto)}}), in the 6th year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}}, the deity {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} enshrined in the {{ill|lt=Imperial Palace|Q500681|WD=Q500681|1=Tokyo Imperial Palace}} was moved to the land of Sasanuhi because of reverence. Shrine tradition states: "On the 3rd day of the 9th month in Emperor Sujin's 6th year, mirrors in the form of the sun image were cast in this place (to enshrine in the palace in place of Amaterasu), and made into the divine spirit of Amaterasu; the mirror now in the inner attendants' office is that one. This shrine enshrines the prototype mirror made at that time as {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411|lt=Amateru Kuniteru Hikohokari-no-Mikoto|1=Amenohoakari}}, and this place is therefore called Kagamitsukuri." It is said that this prototype mirror became the shrine's sacred object, and that {{ill|lt=Ishikoridome|Q6080112|WD=Q6080112|lt=Ishikoridome-no-Mikoto|1=Ishikori-dome no Mikoto}} (who forged the {{ill|lt=Yata no Kagami|Q2610017|WD=Q2610017|1=Yata no Kagami}} when Amaterasu hid in the {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Iwato|Q3231835|WD=Q3231835|1=Amano-Iwato}}) and her father {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Nukado|1=UNKNOWN}} no Mikoto were also enshrined here. The ''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Wamyosho|1=UNKNOWN}}'' records "Kagamitsukuri-go" in {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}, {{ill|lt=Shikige District|Q11486987|WD=Q11486987|lt=Jokage District|1=Shikige district}}. Since the mirror-making clan (kagamitsukuri-be) is said to have lived here producing mirrors, the shrine's founding is thought to be closely tied to that clan. Nearby shrines such as {{ill|lt=Kagamitsukuri Ita Shrine|Q54153278|WD=Q54153278|1=Kagamitsukuri Ita Shrine}} and {{ill|lt=Kagamitsukuri Maki Shrine|Q43594607|WD=Q43594607|1=Kagamitsukurimake Shrine}} are also pointed out as related. [[ファイル:鏡作坐天照御魂神社 御神宝.jpg|サムネイル|右]] In {{ill|lt=Daido (Japan)|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191|lt=Daido|1=Daidō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021|1=806}}), eighteen households were donated as shrine estates (''{{ill|lt=Shinshoku Kofu Sho|Q11502312|WD=Q11502312|1=Shinshō Kyakuchokufushō}}''). In the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'' it is listed as a major shrine and noted to receive offerings for monthly rites and the {{ill|lt=Niiname-sai|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518|lt=new harvest rite|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}}. On {{ill|lt=January 27|Q2284|WD=Q2284|1=January 27}}, {{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an|1=Ten'an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}), it was granted divine rank from junior fifth lower to junior fifth upper (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'') - recorded as "Kagamitsukuri Amateru Mitama no Kami." From the medieval period onward it was called "Kagamitsukuri Sansho Daimyojin," but is said to have declined until the {{ill|lt=Tawaramoto Domain|Q10425374|WD=Q10425374|1=Tawaramoto Domain}} was established in the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}} (then a {{ill|lt=kōtai yoriai|Q11373913|WD=Q11373913|1=kōtai yoriai}} holding of the {{ill|lt=Hirano clan|Q11483690|WD=Q11483690|lt=Hirano family|1=Hirano family}}). From the Edo period onward, it drew deep devotion from the mirror industry. After entering the {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} era and issuance of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinbutsu Bunri Edict|1=UNKNOWN}}, the shrine temple, the {{ill|lt=Shingon sect|Q550182|WD=Q550182|1=Shingon Buddhism}} Kangakurin of Iishu-zan Jingū-ji, was abolished. However, its bell tower alone remained and survives to the present. In April {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} (Meiji 6) it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}, and in {{ill|lt=1936|Q18649|WD=Q18649|1=1936}} ({{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 11) it was ranked as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. It possesses the sacred treasure called "three divine mirrors with two beasts." === Precinct === [[File:Kagamitsukuri-jinja, honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|Main hall]] [[File:Kagamitsukuri-jinja, Otauesai.jpg|thumb|220px|right|Rice-planting festival]] * 3 main halls ** First hall ** Second hall ** Third hall * Haiden * Kagamitsukuri-za {{ill|lt=Wakamiya Shrine|Q11616280|WD=Q11616280|1=Wakamiya Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} Shrine * {{ill|lt=Tajikarao Shrine|Q11496923|WD=Q11496923|1=Tajikarao Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Shrine|Q11381857|WD=Q11381857|1=Sumiyoshi Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Kasuga Shrine|Q10855011|WD=Q10855011|1=Kasuga Shrines}} * Gosha-sha (combined shrine) - {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kasu嶋 Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}, Hachiekanekami Shrine, {{ill|lt=Ukemochi|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323|1=Ukemochi}} Shrine, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Itsukushima Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} Shrine, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sarutahiko|1=UNKNOWN}} Shrine, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōkuninushi|1=UNKNOWN}} Shrine * {{ill|lt=Kotohira Shrine|Q11646488|WD=Q11646488|1=Kotohira shrines}} - stone monument * Sayorihime Shrine * Kagitori Shrine * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Fuefuki Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Aizome Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} * Kagami Pond * Kagami Stone - unearthed from Kagami Pond. It is said to have been used to polish mirror surfaces during mirror making. * Bell tower - bell tower of Jingū-ji Kangakurin. The {{ill|lt=bonsho|Q8192099|WD=Q8192099|1=Bonshō}} was made in {{ill|lt=Kansei|Q1195131|WD=Q1195131|1=Kansei}} 7 ({{ill|lt=1795|Q7815|WD=Q7815|1=1795}}). During the {{ill|lt=Pacific War|Q184425|WD=Q184425|1=Pacific War}} it was surrendered under the {{ill|lt=metal collection order|Q11646956|WD=Q11646956|1=Metal Collection Order}}, but the war ended before it was melted and it was safely returned to the shrine. * Shrine office ファイル:Kagamitsukuri Jinja Haiden.jpg|Haiden ファイル:Kagamitsukuri Jinja Kagamiishi.jpg|Kagami Stone ファイル:Kagamitsukuri Wakamiya Jinja.jpg|Kagamitsukuri Wakamiya Shrine === Access === * 15 minutes on foot northeast from {{ill|lt=Tawaramoto Station|Q7689224|WD=Q7689224|1=Tawaramoto Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu|1=Kintetsu Railway}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kashihara Line|1=UNKNOWN}}. === See also === {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|Q11433427|WD=Q11433427|1=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province}} === References === * Information board by Tawaramoto Board of Education === External links === *[http://www.town.tawaramoto.nara.jp/kanko/bunkazai/temple/3446.html Kagamitsukuri Shrine] - Tawaramoto Town *[http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22807 Kagamitsukuri Nimasu Amateru Mitama Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Compilation of Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials" {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かかみつくりにますあまてるみたましんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kagamitsukuri Nimasu Amateru Mitama Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kagamitsukuri Nimasu Amateru Mitama Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧県社]] [[Category:田原本町の建築物]] [[Category:田原本町の歴史]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qqqqqqq]]

Amateru Mitama Shrine |画像=[[Image:Kagamitsukuri Jinja Torii.jpg|270px]]
Torii |所在地={{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Shiki District|Q1155036|WD=Q1155036|1=Shiki District, Nara}}{{ill|lt=Tawaramoto|Q1203159|WD=Q1203159|1=Tawaramoto, Nara}} Yao 816 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411|lt=Amateru Kuniteru Hikohokari-no-Mikoto|1=Amenohoakari}}

{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Nukado|1=UNKNOWN}} no Mikoto |神体=Three divine mirrors with two beasts |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (major)
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} |創建={{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}} 6 ({{ill|lt=92 BCE|Q42350|WD=Q42350|1=92 BC}}) |本殿=Three-bay shrine, {{ill|lt=kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} |別名=Kagamitsukuri Shrine |例祭={{ill|lt=October 25|Q2954|WD=Q2954|1=October 25}} |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Kagamitsukuri Nimasu Amateru
Mitama Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kagamitsukuri Nimasu Amateru Mitama Shrine''' (かがみつくりにますあまてるみたまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Tawaramoto|Q1203159|WD=Q1203159|1=Tawaramoto, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Shiki District|Q1155036|WD=Q1155036|1=Shiki District, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=major Shikinaisha|Q134917288|WD=Q134917288|1=Shikinai Taisha}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. It is said to have been founded by the Kagamitsukuri-be mirror-making clan, who lived in this area from the 4th to 5th centuries producing mirrors, and enshrined their tutelary deity ({{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411|lt=Amateru Kuniteru Hikohokari-no-Mikoto|1=Amenohoakari}}) together with their ancestor ({{ill|lt=Ishikoridome|Q6080112|WD=Q6080112|lt=Ishikoridome-no-Mikoto|1=Ishikori-dome no Mikoto}}). === Enshrined deities === The three enshrined deities are: * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411|lt=Amateru Kuniteru Hikohokari-no-Mikoto|1=Amenohoakari}} (あまてるくにてるひこほあかりのみこと) * {{ill|lt=Ishikoridome|Q6080112|WD=Q6080112|lt=Ishikoridome-no-Mikoto|1=Ishikori-dome no Mikoto}} (いしこりどめのみこと) * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Nukado|1=UNKNOWN}} no Mikoto (あめのぬかどのみこと) In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Register of Shrines|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, only one deity seat is recorded. Because the shrine name means "the shrine of Amateru Mitama no Kami seated at Kagamitsukuri," it is considered originally a solar-cult shrine devoted to "Amateru Mitama no Kami." In the Jinmyocho, shrines called Amateru Mitama Jinja exist in several regions of {{ill|lt=Japan|Q17|WD=Q17|1=Japan}}; these are regarded as solar deities of a different {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=divine status|1=UNKNOWN}} from {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} (the imperial ancestral deity). === History === According to classical chronicles ({{ill|lt=Kiki|Q842308|WD=Q842308|1=Kiki (Shinto)}}), in the 6th year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}}, the deity {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} enshrined in the {{ill|lt=Imperial Palace|Q500681|WD=Q500681|1=Tokyo Imperial Palace}} was moved to the land of Sasanuhi because of reverence. Shrine tradition states: "On the 3rd day of the 9th month in Emperor Sujin's 6th year, mirrors in the form of the sun image were cast in this place (to enshrine in the palace in place of Amaterasu), and made into the divine spirit of Amaterasu; the mirror now in the inner attendants' office is that one. This shrine enshrines the prototype mirror made at that time as {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411|lt=Amateru Kuniteru Hikohokari-no-Mikoto|1=Amenohoakari}}, and this place is therefore called Kagamitsukuri." It is said that this prototype mirror became the shrine's sacred object, and that {{ill|lt=Ishikoridome|Q6080112|WD=Q6080112|lt=Ishikoridome-no-Mikoto|1=Ishikori-dome no Mikoto}} (who forged the {{ill|lt=Yata no Kagami|Q2610017|WD=Q2610017|1=Yata no Kagami}} when Amaterasu hid in the {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Iwato|Q3231835|WD=Q3231835|1=Amano-Iwato}}) and her father {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Nukado|1=UNKNOWN}} no Mikoto were also enshrined here. The ''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Wamyosho|1=UNKNOWN}}'' records "Kagamitsukuri-go" in {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}, {{ill|lt=Shikige District|Q11486987|WD=Q11486987|lt=Jokage District|1=Shikige district}}. Since the mirror-making clan (kagamitsukuri-be) is said to have lived here producing mirrors, the shrine's founding is thought to be closely tied to that clan. Nearby shrines such as {{ill|lt=Kagamitsukuri Ita Shrine|Q54153278|WD=Q54153278|1=Kagamitsukuri Ita Shrine}} and {{ill|lt=Kagamitsukuri Maki Shrine|Q43594607|WD=Q43594607|1=Kagamitsukurimake Shrine}} are also pointed out as related. [[ファイル:鏡作坐天照御魂神社 御神宝.jpg|サムネイル|右]] In {{ill|lt=Daido (Japan)|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191|lt=Daido|1=Daidō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021|1=806}}), eighteen households were donated as shrine estates (''{{ill|lt=Shinshoku Kofu Sho|Q11502312|WD=Q11502312|1=Shinshō Kyakuchokufushō}}''). In the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'' it is listed as a major shrine and noted to receive offerings for monthly rites and the {{ill|lt=Niiname-sai|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518|lt=new harvest rite|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}}. On {{ill|lt=January 27|Q2284|WD=Q2284|1=January 27}}, {{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an|1=Ten'an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}), it was granted divine rank from junior fifth lower to junior fifth upper (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'') - recorded as "Kagamitsukuri Amateru Mitama no Kami." From the medieval period onward it was called "Kagamitsukuri Sansho Daimyojin," but is said to have declined until the {{ill|lt=Tawaramoto Domain|Q10425374|WD=Q10425374|1=Tawaramoto Domain}} was established in the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}} (then a {{ill|lt=kōtai yoriai|Q11373913|WD=Q11373913|1=kōtai yoriai}} holding of the {{ill|lt=Hirano clan|Q11483690|WD=Q11483690|lt=Hirano family|1=Hirano family}}). From the Edo period onward, it drew deep devotion from the mirror industry. After entering the {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} era and issuance of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinbutsu Bunri Edict|1=UNKNOWN}}, the shrine temple, the {{ill|lt=Shingon sect|Q550182|WD=Q550182|1=Shingon Buddhism}} Kangakurin of Iishu-zan Jingū-ji, was abolished. However, its bell tower alone remained and survives to the present. In April {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} (Meiji 6) it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}, and in {{ill|lt=1936|Q18649|WD=Q18649|1=1936}} ({{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 11) it was ranked as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. It possesses the sacred treasure called "three divine mirrors with two beasts." === Precinct === [[File:Kagamitsukuri-jinja, honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|Main hall]] [[File:Kagamitsukuri-jinja, Otauesai.jpg|thumb|220px|right|Rice-planting festival]] * 3 main halls ** First hall ** Second hall ** Third hall * Haiden * Kagamitsukuri-za {{ill|lt=Wakamiya Shrine|Q11616280|WD=Q11616280|1=Wakamiya Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} Shrine * {{ill|lt=Tajikarao Shrine|Q11496923|WD=Q11496923|1=Tajikarao Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Shrine|Q11381857|WD=Q11381857|1=Sumiyoshi Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Kasuga Shrine|Q10855011|WD=Q10855011|1=Kasuga Shrines}} * Gosha-sha (combined shrine) - {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kasu嶋 Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}, Hachiekanekami Shrine, {{ill|lt=Ukemochi|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323|1=Ukemochi}} Shrine, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Itsukushima Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} Shrine, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sarutahiko|1=UNKNOWN}} Shrine, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōkuninushi|1=UNKNOWN}} Shrine * {{ill|lt=Kotohira Shrine|Q11646488|WD=Q11646488|1=Kotohira shrines}} - stone monument * Sayorihime Shrine * Kagitori Shrine * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Fuefuki Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Aizome Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} * Kagami Pond * Kagami Stone - unearthed from Kagami Pond. It is said to have been used to polish mirror surfaces during mirror making. * Bell tower - bell tower of Jingū-ji Kangakurin. The {{ill|lt=bonsho|Q8192099|WD=Q8192099|1=Bonshō}} was made in {{ill|lt=Kansei|Q1195131|WD=Q1195131|1=Kansei}} 7 ({{ill|lt=1795|Q7815|WD=Q7815|1=1795}}). During the {{ill|lt=Pacific War|Q184425|WD=Q184425|1=Pacific War}} it was surrendered under the {{ill|lt=metal collection order|Q11646956|WD=Q11646956|1=Metal Collection Order}}, but the war ended before it was melted and it was safely returned to the shrine. * Shrine office ファイル:Kagamitsukuri Jinja Haiden.jpg|Haiden ファイル:Kagamitsukuri Jinja Kagamiishi.jpg|Kagami Stone ファイル:Kagamitsukuri Wakamiya Jinja.jpg|Kagamitsukuri Wakamiya Shrine === Access === * 15 minutes on foot northeast from {{ill|lt=Tawaramoto Station|Q7689224|WD=Q7689224|1=Tawaramoto Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu|1=Kintetsu Railway}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kashihara Line|1=UNKNOWN}}. === See also === {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|Q11433427|WD=Q11433427|1=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province}} === References === * Information board by Tawaramoto Board of Education === External links === *[http://www.town.tawaramoto.nara.jp/kanko/bunkazai/temple/3446.html Kagamitsukuri Shrine] - Tawaramoto Town *[http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22807 Kagamitsukuri Nimasu Amateru Mitama Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Compilation of Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials" {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kagamitsukuri Nimasu Amateru Mitama Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kagamitsukuri Nimasu Amateru Mitama Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧県社]] [[Category:田原本町の建築物]] [[Category:田原本町の歴史]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qqqqqqq]]

entrance (P9721) == * Kagamitsukuri Jinja Torii.jpg {{commons category|Category:Kagamitsukuri-jinja}} [[JA:鏡作坐天照御魂神社]] {{wikidata link|Q38278496}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Kagamitsukuri Nimasu Amateru Mitama Shrine |画像=[[Image:Kagamitsukuri Jinja Torii.jpg|270px]]
Torii |所在地={{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Shiki District|Q1155036|WD=Q1155036|1=Shiki District, Nara}}{{ill|lt=Tawaramoto|Q1203159|WD=Q1203159|1=Tawaramoto, Nara}} Yao 816 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411|lt=Amateru Kuniteru Hikohokari-no-Mikoto|1=Amenohoakari}}
{{ill|lt=Ishikoridome|Q6080112|WD=Q6080112|lt=Ishikoridome-no-Mikoto|1=Ishikori-dome no Mikoto}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Nukado|1=UNKNOWN}} no Mikoto |神体=Three divine mirrors with two beasts |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (major)
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} |創建={{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}} 6 ({{ill|lt=92 BCE|Q42350|WD=Q42350|1=92 BC}}) |本殿=Three-bay shrine, {{ill|lt=kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} |別名=Kagamitsukuri Shrine |例祭={{ill|lt=October 25|Q2954|WD=Q2954|1=October 25}} |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Kagamitsukuri Nimasu Amateru
Mitama Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kagamitsukuri Nimasu Amateru Mitama Shrine''' (かがみつくりにますあまてるみたまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Tawaramoto|Q1203159|WD=Q1203159|1=Tawaramoto, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Shiki District|Q1155036|WD=Q1155036|1=Shiki District, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=major Shikinaisha|Q134917288|WD=Q134917288|1=Shikinai Taisha}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. It is said to have been founded by the Kagamitsukuri-be mirror-making clan, who lived in this area from the 4th to 5th centuries producing mirrors, and enshrined their tutelary deity ({{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411|lt=Amateru Kuniteru Hikohokari-no-Mikoto|1=Amenohoakari}}) together with their ancestor ({{ill|lt=Ishikoridome|Q6080112|WD=Q6080112|lt=Ishikoridome-no-Mikoto|1=Ishikori-dome no Mikoto}}). === Enshrined deities === The three enshrined deities are: * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411|lt=Amateru Kuniteru Hikohokari-no-Mikoto|1=Amenohoakari}} (あまてるくにてるひこほあかりのみこと) * {{ill|lt=Ishikoridome|Q6080112|WD=Q6080112|lt=Ishikoridome-no-Mikoto|1=Ishikori-dome no Mikoto}} (いしこりどめのみこと) * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Nukado|1=UNKNOWN}} no Mikoto (あめのぬかどのみこと) In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Register of Shrines|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, only one deity seat is recorded. Because the shrine name means "the shrine of Amateru Mitama no Kami seated at Kagamitsukuri," it is considered originally a solar-cult shrine devoted to "Amateru Mitama no Kami." In the Jinmyocho, shrines called Amateru Mitama Jinja exist in several regions of {{ill|lt=Japan|Q17|WD=Q17|1=Japan}}; these are regarded as solar deities of a different {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=divine status|1=UNKNOWN}} from {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} (the imperial ancestral deity). === History === According to classical chronicles ({{ill|lt=Kiki|Q842308|WD=Q842308|1=Kiki (Shinto)}}), in the 6th year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}}, the deity {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} enshrined in the {{ill|lt=Imperial Palace|Q500681|WD=Q500681|1=Tokyo Imperial Palace}} was moved to the land of Sasanuhi because of reverence. Shrine tradition states: "On the 3rd day of the 9th month in Emperor Sujin's 6th year, mirrors in the form of the sun image were cast in this place (to enshrine in the palace in place of Amaterasu), and made into the divine spirit of Amaterasu; the mirror now in the inner attendants' office is that one. This shrine enshrines the prototype mirror made at that time as {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411|lt=Amateru Kuniteru Hikohokari-no-Mikoto|1=Amenohoakari}}, and this place is therefore called Kagamitsukuri." It is said that this prototype mirror became the shrine's sacred object, and that {{ill|lt=Ishikoridome|Q6080112|WD=Q6080112|lt=Ishikoridome-no-Mikoto|1=Ishikori-dome no Mikoto}} (who forged the {{ill|lt=Yata no Kagami|Q2610017|WD=Q2610017|1=Yata no Kagami}} when Amaterasu hid in the {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Iwato|Q3231835|WD=Q3231835|1=Amano-Iwato}}) and her father {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Nukado|1=UNKNOWN}} no Mikoto were also enshrined here. The ''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Wamyosho|1=UNKNOWN}}'' records "Kagamitsukuri-go" in {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}, {{ill|lt=Shikige District|Q11486987|WD=Q11486987|lt=Jokage District|1=Shikige district}}. Since the mirror-making clan (kagamitsukuri-be) is said to have lived here producing mirrors, the shrine's founding is thought to be closely tied to that clan. Nearby shrines such as {{ill|lt=Kagamitsukuri Ita Shrine|Q54153278|WD=Q54153278|1=Kagamitsukuri Ita Shrine}} and {{ill|lt=Kagamitsukuri Maki Shrine|Q43594607|WD=Q43594607|1=Kagamitsukurimake Shrine}} are also pointed out as related. [[ファイル:鏡作坐天照御魂神社 御神宝.jpg|サムネイル|右]] In {{ill|lt=Daido (Japan)|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191|lt=Daido|1=Daidō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021|1=806}}), eighteen households were donated as shrine estates (''{{ill|lt=Shinshoku Kofu Sho|Q11502312|WD=Q11502312|1=Shinshō Kyakuchokufushō}}''). In the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'' it is listed as a major shrine and noted to receive offerings for monthly rites and the {{ill|lt=Niiname-sai|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518|lt=new harvest rite|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}}. On {{ill|lt=January 27|Q2284|WD=Q2284|1=January 27}}, {{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an|1=Ten'an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}), it was granted divine rank from junior fifth lower to junior fifth upper (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'') - recorded as "Kagamitsukuri Amateru Mitama no Kami." From the medieval period onward it was called "Kagamitsukuri Sansho Daimyojin," but is said to have declined until the {{ill|lt=Tawaramoto Domain|Q10425374|WD=Q10425374|1=Tawaramoto Domain}} was established in the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}} (then a {{ill|lt=kōtai yoriai|Q11373913|WD=Q11373913|1=kōtai yoriai}} holding of the {{ill|lt=Hirano clan|Q11483690|WD=Q11483690|lt=Hirano family|1=Hirano family}}). From the Edo period onward, it drew deep devotion from the mirror industry. After entering the {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} era and issuance of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinbutsu Bunri Edict|1=UNKNOWN}}, the shrine temple, the {{ill|lt=Shingon sect|Q550182|WD=Q550182|1=Shingon Buddhism}} Kangakurin of Iishu-zan Jingū-ji, was abolished. However, its bell tower alone remained and survives to the present. In April {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} (Meiji 6) it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}, and in {{ill|lt=1936|Q18649|WD=Q18649|1=1936}} ({{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 11) it was ranked as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. It possesses the sacred treasure called "three divine mirrors with two beasts." === Precinct === [[File:Kagamitsukuri-jinja, honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|Main hall]] [[File:Kagamitsukuri-jinja, Otauesai.jpg|thumb|220px|right|Rice-planting festival]] * 3 main halls ** First hall ** Second hall ** Third hall * Haiden * Kagamitsukuri-za {{ill|lt=Wakamiya Shrine|Q11616280|WD=Q11616280|1=Wakamiya Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} Shrine * {{ill|lt=Tajikarao Shrine|Q11496923|WD=Q11496923|1=Tajikarao Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Shrine|Q11381857|WD=Q11381857|1=Sumiyoshi Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Kasuga Shrine|Q10855011|WD=Q10855011|1=Kasuga Shrines}} * Gosha-sha (combined shrine) - {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kasu嶋 Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}, Hachiekanekami Shrine, {{ill|lt=Ukemochi|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323|1=Ukemochi}} Shrine, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Itsukushima Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} Shrine, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sarutahiko|1=UNKNOWN}} Shrine, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōkuninushi|1=UNKNOWN}} Shrine * {{ill|lt=Kotohira Shrine|Q11646488|WD=Q11646488|1=Kotohira shrines}} - stone monument * Sayorihime Shrine * Kagitori Shrine * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Fuefuki Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Aizome Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} * Kagami Pond * Kagami Stone - unearthed from Kagami Pond. It is said to have been used to polish mirror surfaces during mirror making. * Bell tower - bell tower of Jingū-ji Kangakurin. The {{ill|lt=bonsho|Q8192099|WD=Q8192099|1=Bonshō}} was made in {{ill|lt=Kansei|Q1195131|WD=Q1195131|1=Kansei}} 7 ({{ill|lt=1795|Q7815|WD=Q7815|1=1795}}). During the {{ill|lt=Pacific War|Q184425|WD=Q184425|1=Pacific War}} it was surrendered under the {{ill|lt=metal collection order|Q11646956|WD=Q11646956|1=Metal Collection Order}}, but the war ended before it was melted and it was safely returned to the shrine. * Shrine office ファイル:Kagamitsukuri Jinja Haiden.jpg|Haiden ファイル:Kagamitsukuri Jinja Kagamiishi.jpg|Kagami Stone ファイル:Kagamitsukuri Wakamiya Jinja.jpg|Kagamitsukuri Wakamiya Shrine === Access === * 15 minutes on foot northeast from {{ill|lt=Tawaramoto Station|Q7689224|WD=Q7689224|1=Tawaramoto Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu|1=Kintetsu Railway}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kashihara Line|1=UNKNOWN}}. === See also === {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|Q11433427|WD=Q11433427|1=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province}} === References === * Information board by Tawaramoto Board of Education === External links === *[http://www.town.tawaramoto.nara.jp/kanko/bunkazai/temple/3446.html Kagamitsukuri Shrine] - Tawaramoto Town *[http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22807 Kagamitsukuri Nimasu Amateru Mitama Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Compilation of Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials" {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kagamitsukuri Nimasu Amateru Mitama Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kagamitsukuri Nimasu Amateru Mitama Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧県社]] [[Category:田原本町の建築物]] [[Category:田原本町の歴史]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qqqqqqq]]

Amateru Mitama Shrine |画像=[[Image:Kagamitsukuri Jinja Torii.jpg|270px]]
Torii |所在地={{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Shiki District|Q1155036|WD=Q1155036|1=Shiki District, Nara}}{{ill|lt=Tawaramoto|Q1203159|WD=Q1203159|1=Tawaramoto, Nara}} Yao 816 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411|lt=Amateru Kuniteru Hikohokari-no-Mikoto|1=Amenohoakari}}
{{ill|lt=Ishikoridome|Q6080112|WD=Q6080112|lt=Ishikoridome-no-Mikoto|1=Ishikori-dome no Mikoto}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Nukado|1=UNKNOWN}} no Mikoto |神体=Three divine mirrors with two beasts |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (major)
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} |創建={{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}} 6 ({{ill|lt=92 BCE|Q42350|WD=Q42350|1=92 BC}}) |本殿=Three-bay shrine, {{ill|lt=kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} |別名=Kagamitsukuri Shrine |例祭={{ill|lt=October 25|Q2954|WD=Q2954|1=October 25}} |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Kagamitsukuri Nimasu Amateru
Mitama Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kagamitsukuri Nimasu Amateru Mitama Shrine''' (かがみつくりにますあまてるみたまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Tawaramoto|Q1203159|WD=Q1203159|1=Tawaramoto, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Shiki District|Q1155036|WD=Q1155036|1=Shiki District, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=major Shikinaisha|Q134917288|WD=Q134917288|1=Shikinai Taisha}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. It is said to have been founded by the Kagamitsukuri-be mirror-making clan, who lived in this area from the 4th to 5th centuries producing mirrors, and enshrined their tutelary deity ({{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411|lt=Amateru Kuniteru Hikohokari-no-Mikoto|1=Amenohoakari}}) together with their ancestor ({{ill|lt=Ishikoridome|Q6080112|WD=Q6080112|lt=Ishikoridome-no-Mikoto|1=Ishikori-dome no Mikoto}}). === Enshrined deities === The three enshrined deities are: * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411|lt=Amateru Kuniteru Hikohokari-no-Mikoto|1=Amenohoakari}} (あまてるくにてるひこほあかりのみこと) * {{ill|lt=Ishikoridome|Q6080112|WD=Q6080112|lt=Ishikoridome-no-Mikoto|1=Ishikori-dome no Mikoto}} (いしこりどめのみこと) * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Nukado|1=UNKNOWN}} no Mikoto (あめのぬかどのみこと) In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Register of Shrines|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, only one deity seat is recorded. Because the shrine name means "the shrine of Amateru Mitama no Kami seated at Kagamitsukuri," it is considered originally a solar-cult shrine devoted to "Amateru Mitama no Kami." In the Jinmyocho, shrines called Amateru Mitama Jinja exist in several regions of {{ill|lt=Japan|Q17|WD=Q17|1=Japan}}; these are regarded as solar deities of a different {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=divine status|1=UNKNOWN}} from {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} (the imperial ancestral deity). === History === According to classical chronicles ({{ill|lt=Kiki|Q842308|WD=Q842308|1=Kiki (Shinto)}}), in the 6th year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}}, the deity {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} enshrined in the {{ill|lt=Imperial Palace|Q500681|WD=Q500681|1=Tokyo Imperial Palace}} was moved to the land of Sasanuhi because of reverence. Shrine tradition states: "On the 3rd day of the 9th month in Emperor Sujin's 6th year, mirrors in the form of the sun image were cast in this place (to enshrine in the palace in place of Amaterasu), and made into the divine spirit of Amaterasu; the mirror now in the inner attendants' office is that one. This shrine enshrines the prototype mirror made at that time as {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411|lt=Amateru Kuniteru Hikohokari-no-Mikoto|1=Amenohoakari}}, and this place is therefore called Kagamitsukuri." It is said that this prototype mirror became the shrine's sacred object, and that {{ill|lt=Ishikoridome|Q6080112|WD=Q6080112|lt=Ishikoridome-no-Mikoto|1=Ishikori-dome no Mikoto}} (who forged the {{ill|lt=Yata no Kagami|Q2610017|WD=Q2610017|1=Yata no Kagami}} when Amaterasu hid in the {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Iwato|Q3231835|WD=Q3231835|1=Amano-Iwato}}) and her father {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Nukado|1=UNKNOWN}} no Mikoto were also enshrined here. The ''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Wamyosho|1=UNKNOWN}}'' records "Kagamitsukuri-go" in {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}, {{ill|lt=Shikige District|Q11486987|WD=Q11486987|lt=Jokage District|1=Shikige district}}. Since the mirror-making clan (kagamitsukuri-be) is said to have lived here producing mirrors, the shrine's founding is thought to be closely tied to that clan. Nearby shrines such as {{ill|lt=Kagamitsukuri Ita Shrine|Q54153278|WD=Q54153278|1=Kagamitsukuri Ita Shrine}} and {{ill|lt=Kagamitsukuri Maki Shrine|Q43594607|WD=Q43594607|1=Kagamitsukurimake Shrine}} are also pointed out as related. [[ファイル:鏡作坐天照御魂神社 御神宝.jpg|サムネイル|右]] In {{ill|lt=Daido (Japan)|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191|lt=Daido|1=Daidō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021|1=806}}), eighteen households were donated as shrine estates (''{{ill|lt=Shinshoku Kofu Sho|Q11502312|WD=Q11502312|1=Shinshō Kyakuchokufushō}}''). In the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'' it is listed as a major shrine and noted to receive offerings for monthly rites and the {{ill|lt=Niiname-sai|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518|lt=new harvest rite|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}}. On {{ill|lt=January 27|Q2284|WD=Q2284|1=January 27}}, {{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an|1=Ten'an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}), it was granted divine rank from junior fifth lower to junior fifth upper (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'') - recorded as "Kagamitsukuri Amateru Mitama no Kami." From the medieval period onward it was called "Kagamitsukuri Sansho Daimyojin," but is said to have declined until the {{ill|lt=Tawaramoto Domain|Q10425374|WD=Q10425374|1=Tawaramoto Domain}} was established in the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}} (then a {{ill|lt=kōtai yoriai|Q11373913|WD=Q11373913|1=kōtai yoriai}} holding of the {{ill|lt=Hirano clan|Q11483690|WD=Q11483690|lt=Hirano family|1=Hirano family}}). From the Edo period onward, it drew deep devotion from the mirror industry. After entering the {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} era and issuance of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinbutsu Bunri Edict|1=UNKNOWN}}, the shrine temple, the {{ill|lt=Shingon sect|Q550182|WD=Q550182|1=Shingon Buddhism}} Kangakurin of Iishu-zan Jingū-ji, was abolished. However, its bell tower alone remained and survives to the present. In April {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} (Meiji 6) it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}, and in {{ill|lt=1936|Q18649|WD=Q18649|1=1936}} ({{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 11) it was ranked as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. It possesses the sacred treasure called "three divine mirrors with two beasts." === Precinct === [[File:Kagamitsukuri-jinja, honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|Main hall]] [[File:Kagamitsukuri-jinja, Otauesai.jpg|thumb|220px|right|Rice-planting festival]] * 3 main halls ** First hall ** Second hall ** Third hall * Haiden * Kagamitsukuri-za {{ill|lt=Wakamiya Shrine|Q11616280|WD=Q11616280|1=Wakamiya Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} Shrine * {{ill|lt=Tajikarao Shrine|Q11496923|WD=Q11496923|1=Tajikarao Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Shrine|Q11381857|WD=Q11381857|1=Sumiyoshi Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Kasuga Shrine|Q10855011|WD=Q10855011|1=Kasuga Shrines}} * Gosha-sha (combined shrine) - {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kasu嶋 Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}, Hachiekanekami Shrine, {{ill|lt=Ukemochi|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323|1=Ukemochi}} Shrine, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Itsukushima Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} Shrine, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sarutahiko|1=UNKNOWN}} Shrine, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōkuninushi|1=UNKNOWN}} Shrine * {{ill|lt=Kotohira Shrine|Q11646488|WD=Q11646488|1=Kotohira shrines}} - stone monument * Sayorihime Shrine * Kagitori Shrine * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Fuefuki Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Aizome Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} * Kagami Pond * Kagami Stone - unearthed from Kagami Pond. It is said to have been used to polish mirror surfaces during mirror making. * Bell tower - bell tower of Jingū-ji Kangakurin. The {{ill|lt=bonsho|Q8192099|WD=Q8192099|1=Bonshō}} was made in {{ill|lt=Kansei|Q1195131|WD=Q1195131|1=Kansei}} 7 ({{ill|lt=1795|Q7815|WD=Q7815|1=1795}}). During the {{ill|lt=Pacific War|Q184425|WD=Q184425|1=Pacific War}} it was surrendered under the {{ill|lt=metal collection order|Q11646956|WD=Q11646956|1=Metal Collection Order}}, but the war ended before it was melted and it was safely returned to the shrine. * Shrine office ファイル:Kagamitsukuri Jinja Haiden.jpg|Haiden ファイル:Kagamitsukuri Jinja Kagamiishi.jpg|Kagami Stone ファイル:Kagamitsukuri Wakamiya Jinja.jpg|Kagamitsukuri Wakamiya Shrine === Access === * 15 minutes on foot northeast from {{ill|lt=Tawaramoto Station|Q7689224|WD=Q7689224|1=Tawaramoto Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu|1=Kintetsu Railway}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kashihara Line|1=UNKNOWN}}. === See also === {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|Q11433427|WD=Q11433427|1=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province}} === References === * Information board by Tawaramoto Board of Education === External links === *[http://www.town.tawaramoto.nara.jp/kanko/bunkazai/temple/3446.html Kagamitsukuri Shrine] - Tawaramoto Town *[http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22807 Kagamitsukuri Nimasu Amateru Mitama Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Compilation of Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials" {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kagamitsukuri Nimasu Amateru Mitama Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kagamitsukuri Nimasu Amateru Mitama Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:田原本町の建築物]] [[Category:田原本町の歴史]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qqqqqqq]]

Amateru Mitama Shrine |画像=[[Image:Kagamitsukuri Jinja Torii.jpg|270px]]
Torii |所在地={{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Shiki District|Q1155036|WD=Q1155036|1=Shiki District, Nara}}{{ill|lt=Tawaramoto|Q1203159|WD=Q1203159|1=Tawaramoto, Nara}} Yao 816 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411|lt=Amateru Kuniteru Hikohokari-no-Mikoto|1=Amenohoakari}}
{{ill|lt=Ishikoridome|Q6080112|WD=Q6080112|lt=Ishikoridome-no-Mikoto|1=Ishikori-dome no Mikoto}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Nukado|1=UNKNOWN}} no Mikoto |神体=Three divine mirrors with two beasts |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (major)
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} |創建={{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}} 6 ({{ill|lt=92 BCE|Q42350|WD=Q42350|1=92 BC}}) |本殿=Three-bay shrine, {{ill|lt=kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} |別名=Kagamitsukuri Shrine |例祭={{ill|lt=October 25|Q2954|WD=Q2954|1=October 25}} |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Kagamitsukuri Nimasu Amateru
Mitama Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kagamitsukuri Nimasu Amateru Mitama Shrine''' (かがみつくりにますあまてるみたまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Tawaramoto|Q1203159|WD=Q1203159|1=Tawaramoto, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Shiki District|Q1155036|WD=Q1155036|1=Shiki District, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=major Shikinaisha|Q134917288|WD=Q134917288|1=Shikinai Taisha}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. It is said to have been founded by the Kagamitsukuri-be mirror-making clan, who lived in this area from the 4th to 5th centuries producing mirrors, and enshrined their tutelary deity ({{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411|lt=Amateru Kuniteru Hikohokari-no-Mikoto|1=Amenohoakari}}) together with their ancestor ({{ill|lt=Ishikoridome|Q6080112|WD=Q6080112|lt=Ishikoridome-no-Mikoto|1=Ishikori-dome no Mikoto}}). === Enshrined deities === The three enshrined deities are: * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411|lt=Amateru Kuniteru Hikohokari-no-Mikoto|1=Amenohoakari}} (あまてるくにてるひこほあかりのみこと) * {{ill|lt=Ishikoridome|Q6080112|WD=Q6080112|lt=Ishikoridome-no-Mikoto|1=Ishikori-dome no Mikoto}} (いしこりどめのみこと) * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Nukado|1=UNKNOWN}} no Mikoto (あめのぬかどのみこと) In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Register of Shrines|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, only one deity seat is recorded. Because the shrine name means "the shrine of Amateru Mitama no Kami seated at Kagamitsukuri," it is considered originally a solar-cult shrine devoted to "Amateru Mitama no Kami." In the Jinmyocho, shrines called Amateru Mitama Jinja exist in several regions of {{ill|lt=Japan|Q17|WD=Q17|1=Japan}}; these are regarded as solar deities of a different {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=divine status|1=UNKNOWN}} from {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} (the imperial ancestral deity). === History === According to classical chronicles ({{ill|lt=Kiki|Q842308|WD=Q842308|1=Kiki (Shinto)}}), in the 6th year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}}, the deity {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} enshrined in the {{ill|lt=Imperial Palace|Q500681|WD=Q500681|1=Tokyo Imperial Palace}} was moved to the land of Sasanuhi because of reverence. Shrine tradition states: "On the 3rd day of the 9th month in Emperor Sujin's 6th year, mirrors in the form of the sun image were cast in this place (to enshrine in the palace in place of Amaterasu), and made into the divine spirit of Amaterasu; the mirror now in the inner attendants' office is that one. This shrine enshrines the prototype mirror made at that time as {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411|lt=Amateru Kuniteru Hikohokari-no-Mikoto|1=Amenohoakari}}, and this place is therefore called Kagamitsukuri." It is said that this prototype mirror became the shrine's sacred object, and that {{ill|lt=Ishikoridome|Q6080112|WD=Q6080112|lt=Ishikoridome-no-Mikoto|1=Ishikori-dome no Mikoto}} (who forged the {{ill|lt=Yata no Kagami|Q2610017|WD=Q2610017|1=Yata no Kagami}} when Amaterasu hid in the {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Iwato|Q3231835|WD=Q3231835|1=Amano-Iwato}}) and her father {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Nukado|1=UNKNOWN}} no Mikoto were also enshrined here. The ''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Wamyosho|1=UNKNOWN}}'' records "Kagamitsukuri-go" in {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}, {{ill|lt=Shikige District|Q11486987|WD=Q11486987|lt=Jokage District|1=Shikige district}}. Since the mirror-making clan (kagamitsukuri-be) is said to have lived here producing mirrors, the shrine's founding is thought to be closely tied to that clan. Nearby shrines such as {{ill|lt=Kagamitsukuri Ita Shrine|Q54153278|WD=Q54153278|1=Kagamitsukuri Ita Shrine}} and {{ill|lt=Kagamitsukuri Maki Shrine|Q43594607|WD=Q43594607|1=Kagamitsukurimake Shrine}} are also pointed out as related. [[ファイル:鏡作坐天照御魂神社 御神宝.jpg|サムネイル|右]] In {{ill|lt=Daido (Japan)|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191|lt=Daido|1=Daidō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021|1=806}}), eighteen households were donated as shrine estates (''{{ill|lt=Shinshoku Kofu Sho|Q11502312|WD=Q11502312|1=Shinshō Kyakuchokufushō}}''). In the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'' it is listed as a major shrine and noted to receive offerings for monthly rites and the {{ill|lt=Niiname-sai|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518|lt=new harvest rite|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}}. On {{ill|lt=January 27|Q2284|WD=Q2284|1=January 27}}, {{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an|1=Ten'an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}), it was granted divine rank from junior fifth lower to junior fifth upper (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'') - recorded as "Kagamitsukuri Amateru Mitama no Kami." From the medieval period onward it was called "Kagamitsukuri Sansho Daimyojin," but is said to have declined until the {{ill|lt=Tawaramoto Domain|Q10425374|WD=Q10425374|1=Tawaramoto Domain}} was established in the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}} (then a {{ill|lt=kōtai yoriai|Q11373913|WD=Q11373913|1=kōtai yoriai}} holding of the {{ill|lt=Hirano clan|Q11483690|WD=Q11483690|lt=Hirano family|1=Hirano family}}). From the Edo period onward, it drew deep devotion from the mirror industry. After entering the {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} era and issuance of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinbutsu Bunri Edict|1=UNKNOWN}}, the shrine temple, the {{ill|lt=Shingon sect|Q550182|WD=Q550182|1=Shingon Buddhism}} Kangakurin of Iishu-zan Jingū-ji, was abolished. However, its bell tower alone remained and survives to the present. In April {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} (Meiji 6) it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}, and in {{ill|lt=1936|Q18649|WD=Q18649|1=1936}} ({{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 11) it was ranked as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. It possesses the sacred treasure called "three divine mirrors with two beasts." === Precinct === [[File:Kagamitsukuri-jinja, honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|Main hall]] [[File:Kagamitsukuri-jinja, Otauesai.jpg|thumb|220px|right|Rice-planting festival]] * 3 main halls ** First hall ** Second hall ** Third hall * Haiden * Kagamitsukuri-za {{ill|lt=Wakamiya Shrine|Q11616280|WD=Q11616280|1=Wakamiya Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} Shrine * {{ill|lt=Tajikarao Shrine|Q11496923|WD=Q11496923|1=Tajikarao Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Shrine|Q11381857|WD=Q11381857|1=Sumiyoshi Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Kasuga Shrine|Q10855011|WD=Q10855011|1=Kasuga Shrines}} * Gosha-sha (combined shrine) - {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kasu嶋 Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}, Hachiekanekami Shrine, {{ill|lt=Ukemochi|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323|1=Ukemochi}} Shrine, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Itsukushima Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} Shrine, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sarutahiko|1=UNKNOWN}} Shrine, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōkuninushi|1=UNKNOWN}} Shrine * {{ill|lt=Kotohira Shrine|Q11646488|WD=Q11646488|1=Kotohira shrines}} - stone monument * Sayorihime Shrine * Kagitori Shrine * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Fuefuki Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Aizome Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} * Kagami Pond * Kagami Stone - unearthed from Kagami Pond. It is said to have been used to polish mirror surfaces during mirror making. * Bell tower - bell tower of Jingū-ji Kangakurin. The {{ill|lt=bonsho|Q8192099|WD=Q8192099|1=Bonshō}} was made in {{ill|lt=Kansei|Q1195131|WD=Q1195131|1=Kansei}} 7 ({{ill|lt=1795|Q7815|WD=Q7815|1=1795}}). During the {{ill|lt=Pacific War|Q184425|WD=Q184425|1=Pacific War}} it was surrendered under the {{ill|lt=metal collection order|Q11646956|WD=Q11646956|1=Metal Collection Order}}, but the war ended before it was melted and it was safely returned to the shrine. * Shrine office ファイル:Kagamitsukuri Jinja Haiden.jpg|Haiden ファイル:Kagamitsukuri Jinja Kagamiishi.jpg|Kagami Stone ファイル:Kagamitsukuri Wakamiya Jinja.jpg|Kagamitsukuri Wakamiya Shrine === Access === * 15 minutes on foot northeast from {{ill|lt=Tawaramoto Station|Q7689224|WD=Q7689224|1=Tawaramoto Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu|1=Kintetsu Railway}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kashihara Line|1=UNKNOWN}}. === See also === {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|Q11433427|WD=Q11433427|1=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province}} === References === * Information board by Tawaramoto Board of Education === External links === *[http://www.town.tawaramoto.nara.jp/kanko/bunkazai/temple/3446.html Kagamitsukuri Shrine] - Tawaramoto Town *[http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22807 Kagamitsukuri Nimasu Amateru Mitama Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Compilation of Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials" {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kagamitsukuri Nimasu Amateru Mitama Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kagamitsukuri Nimasu Amateru Mitama Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:田原本町の建築物]] [[Category:田原本町の歴史]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qqqqqqq]]

Amateru Mitama Shrine |画像=[[Image:Kagamitsukuri Jinja Torii.jpg|270px]]
Torii |所在地={{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Shiki District|Q1155036|WD=Q1155036|1=Shiki District, Nara}}{{ill|lt=Tawaramoto|Q1203159|WD=Q1203159|1=Tawaramoto, Nara}} Yao 816 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411|lt=Amateru Kuniteru Hikohokari-no-Mikoto|1=Amenohoakari}}
{{ill|lt=Ishikoridome|Q6080112|WD=Q6080112|lt=Ishikoridome-no-Mikoto|1=Ishikori-dome no Mikoto}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Nukado|1=UNKNOWN}} no Mikoto |神体=Three divine mirrors with two beasts |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (major)
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} |創建={{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}} 6 ({{ill|lt=92 BCE|Q42350|WD=Q42350|1=92 BC}}) |本殿=Three-bay shrine, {{ill|lt=kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} |別名=Kagamitsukuri Shrine |例祭={{ill|lt=October 25|Q2954|WD=Q2954|1=October 25}} |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Kagamitsukuri Nimasu Amateru
Mitama Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kagamitsukuri Nimasu Amateru Mitama Shrine''' (かがみつくりにますあまてるみたまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Tawaramoto|Q1203159|WD=Q1203159|1=Tawaramoto, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Shiki District|Q1155036|WD=Q1155036|1=Shiki District, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=major Shikinaisha|Q134917288|WD=Q134917288|1=Shikinai Taisha}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. It is said to have been founded by the Kagamitsukuri-be mirror-making clan, who lived in this area from the 4th to 5th centuries producing mirrors, and enshrined their tutelary deity ({{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411|lt=Amateru Kuniteru Hikohokari-no-Mikoto|1=Amenohoakari}}) together with their ancestor ({{ill|lt=Ishikoridome|Q6080112|WD=Q6080112|lt=Ishikoridome-no-Mikoto|1=Ishikori-dome no Mikoto}}). === Enshrined deities === The three enshrined deities are: * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411|lt=Amateru Kuniteru Hikohokari-no-Mikoto|1=Amenohoakari}} (あまてるくにてるひこほあかりのみこと) * {{ill|lt=Ishikoridome|Q6080112|WD=Q6080112|lt=Ishikoridome-no-Mikoto|1=Ishikori-dome no Mikoto}} (いしこりどめのみこと) * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Nukado|1=UNKNOWN}} no Mikoto (あめのぬかどのみこと) In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Register of Shrines|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, only one deity seat is recorded. Because the shrine name means "the shrine of Amateru Mitama no Kami seated at Kagamitsukuri," it is considered originally a solar-cult shrine devoted to "Amateru Mitama no Kami." In the Jinmyocho, shrines called Amateru Mitama Jinja exist in several regions of {{ill|lt=Japan|Q17|WD=Q17|1=Japan}}; these are regarded as solar deities of a different {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=divine status|1=UNKNOWN}} from {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} (the imperial ancestral deity). === History === According to classical chronicles ({{ill|lt=Kiki|Q842308|WD=Q842308|1=Kiki (Shinto)}}), in the 6th year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}}, the deity {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} enshrined in the {{ill|lt=Imperial Palace|Q500681|WD=Q500681|1=Tokyo Imperial Palace}} was moved to the land of Sasanuhi because of reverence. Shrine tradition states: "On the 3rd day of the 9th month in Emperor Sujin's 6th year, mirrors in the form of the sun image were cast in this place (to enshrine in the palace in place of Amaterasu), and made into the divine spirit of Amaterasu; the mirror now in the inner attendants' office is that one. This shrine enshrines the prototype mirror made at that time as {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411|lt=Amateru Kuniteru Hikohokari-no-Mikoto|1=Amenohoakari}}, and this place is therefore called Kagamitsukuri." It is said that this prototype mirror became the shrine's sacred object, and that {{ill|lt=Ishikoridome|Q6080112|WD=Q6080112|lt=Ishikoridome-no-Mikoto|1=Ishikori-dome no Mikoto}} (who forged the {{ill|lt=Yata no Kagami|Q2610017|WD=Q2610017|1=Yata no Kagami}} when Amaterasu hid in the {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Iwato|Q3231835|WD=Q3231835|1=Amano-Iwato}}) and her father {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Nukado|1=UNKNOWN}} no Mikoto were also enshrined here. The ''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Wamyosho|1=UNKNOWN}}'' records "Kagamitsukuri-go" in {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}, {{ill|lt=Shikige District|Q11486987|WD=Q11486987|lt=Jokage District|1=Shikige district}}. Since the mirror-making clan (kagamitsukuri-be) is said to have lived here producing mirrors, the shrine's founding is thought to be closely tied to that clan. Nearby shrines such as {{ill|lt=Kagamitsukuri Ita Shrine|Q54153278|WD=Q54153278|1=Kagamitsukuri Ita Shrine}} and {{ill|lt=Kagamitsukuri Maki Shrine|Q43594607|WD=Q43594607|1=Kagamitsukurimake Shrine}} are also pointed out as related. [[ファイル:鏡作坐天照御魂神社 御神宝.jpg|サムネイル|右]] In {{ill|lt=Daido (Japan)|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191|lt=Daido|1=Daidō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021|1=806}}), eighteen households were donated as shrine estates (''{{ill|lt=Shinshoku Kofu Sho|Q11502312|WD=Q11502312|1=Shinshō Kyakuchokufushō}}''). In the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'' it is listed as a major shrine and noted to receive offerings for monthly rites and the {{ill|lt=Niiname-sai|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518|lt=new harvest rite|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}}. On {{ill|lt=January 27|Q2284|WD=Q2284|1=January 27}}, {{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an|1=Ten'an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}), it was granted divine rank from junior fifth lower to junior fifth upper (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'') - recorded as "Kagamitsukuri Amateru Mitama no Kami." From the medieval period onward it was called "Kagamitsukuri Sansho Daimyojin," but is said to have declined until the {{ill|lt=Tawaramoto Domain|Q10425374|WD=Q10425374|1=Tawaramoto Domain}} was established in the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}} (then a {{ill|lt=kōtai yoriai|Q11373913|WD=Q11373913|1=kōtai yoriai}} holding of the {{ill|lt=Hirano clan|Q11483690|WD=Q11483690|lt=Hirano family|1=Hirano family}}). From the Edo period onward, it drew deep devotion from the mirror industry. After entering the {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} era and issuance of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinbutsu Bunri Edict|1=UNKNOWN}}, the shrine temple, the {{ill|lt=Shingon sect|Q550182|WD=Q550182|1=Shingon Buddhism}} Kangakurin of Iishu-zan Jingū-ji, was abolished. However, its bell tower alone remained and survives to the present. In April {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} (Meiji 6) it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}, and in {{ill|lt=1936|Q18649|WD=Q18649|1=1936}} ({{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 11) it was ranked as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}. It possesses the sacred treasure called "three divine mirrors with two beasts." === Precinct === [[File:Kagamitsukuri-jinja, honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|Main hall]] [[File:Kagamitsukuri-jinja, Otauesai.jpg|thumb|220px|right|Rice-planting festival]] * 3 main halls ** First hall ** Second hall ** Third hall * Haiden * Kagamitsukuri-za {{ill|lt=Wakamiya Shrine|Q11616280|WD=Q11616280|1=Wakamiya Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} Shrine * {{ill|lt=Tajikarao Shrine|Q11496923|WD=Q11496923|1=Tajikarao Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Shrine|Q11381857|WD=Q11381857|1=Sumiyoshi Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Kasuga Shrine|Q10855011|WD=Q10855011|1=Kasuga Shrines}} * Gosha-sha (combined shrine) - {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kasu嶋 Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}, Hachiekanekami Shrine, {{ill|lt=Ukemochi|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323|1=Ukemochi}} Shrine, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Itsukushima Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} Shrine, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sarutahiko|1=UNKNOWN}} Shrine, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōkuninushi|1=UNKNOWN}} Shrine * {{ill|lt=Kotohira Shrine|Q11646488|WD=Q11646488|1=Kotohira shrines}} - stone monument * Sayorihime Shrine * Kagitori Shrine * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Fuefuki Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Aizome Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} * Kagami Pond * Kagami Stone - unearthed from Kagami Pond. It is said to have been used to polish mirror surfaces during mirror making. * Bell tower - bell tower of Jingū-ji Kangakurin. The {{ill|lt=bonsho|Q8192099|WD=Q8192099|1=Bonshō}} was made in {{ill|lt=Kansei|Q1195131|WD=Q1195131|1=Kansei}} 7 ({{ill|lt=1795|Q7815|WD=Q7815|1=1795}}). During the {{ill|lt=Pacific War|Q184425|WD=Q184425|1=Pacific War}} it was surrendered under the {{ill|lt=metal collection order|Q11646956|WD=Q11646956|1=Metal Collection Order}}, but the war ended before it was melted and it was safely returned to the shrine. * Shrine office ファイル:Kagamitsukuri Jinja Haiden.jpg|Haiden ファイル:Kagamitsukuri Jinja Kagamiishi.jpg|Kagami Stone ファイル:Kagamitsukuri Wakamiya Jinja.jpg|Kagamitsukuri Wakamiya Shrine === Access === * 15 minutes on foot northeast from {{ill|lt=Tawaramoto Station|Q7689224|WD=Q7689224|1=Tawaramoto Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu|1=Kintetsu Railway}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kashihara Line|1=UNKNOWN}}. === See also === {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|Q11433427|WD=Q11433427|1=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province}} === References === * Information board by Tawaramoto Board of Education === External links === *[http://www.town.tawaramoto.nara.jp/kanko/bunkazai/temple/3446.html Kagamitsukuri Shrine] - Tawaramoto Town *[http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22807 Kagamitsukuri Nimasu Amateru Mitama Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Compilation of Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials" {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kagamitsukuri Nimasu Amateru Mitama Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kagamitsukuri Nimasu Amateru Mitama Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:田原本町の建築物]] [[Category:田原本町の歴史]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qqqqqqq]]

Amateru Mitama Shrine |画像=[[Image:Kagamitsukuri Jinja Torii.jpg|270px]]
Torii |所在地={{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Shiki District|Q1155036|WD=Q1155036|1=Shiki District, Nara}}{{ill|lt=Tawaramoto|Q1203159|WD=Q1203159|1=Tawaramoto, Nara}} Yao 816 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411|lt=Amateru Kuniteru Hikohokari-no-Mikoto|1=Amenohoakari}}
